revision.text:
'''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社, Ōyamazumi Jinja)
Ōyamazumi Shrine is a [[Shinto shrines]][[Category:shrine disambiguations]] dedicated to {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|オオヤマツミ}}, associated with the [[Mishima and Ōyamazumi faith]]. As of 1973, 897 shrines existed across Japan, from [[Hokkaido]] to [[Kagoshima Prefecture]]. According to a 1995 survey by the Association of Shinto Shrines, there were 293 shrines, while a 2019 study by [[Kanazawa Institute of Technology]] reported 960 shrines. The shrine may also be written as '''Ōyamazumi Shrine''', '''Ōyamatsumi Shrine''', '''Yamazumi Shrine''', or '''Yamatsumi Shrine'''.
The head shrine is the {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社 (今治市大三島)|Ōyamazumi Shrine=Ōyamazumi Shrine|lt=Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ōmishima)}} in [[Ōmishima]], [[Imabari]], [[Ehime Prefecture]]. Other related shrine names under the [[Mishima and Ōyamazumi faith]] include {{ill|Mishima Shrine|ja|三島神社}}.
== Shrines with Articles == * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishiaizu)|ja|大山祇神社 (西会津町)}} (Nishiaizu, Fukushima Prefecture) * {{ill|Yamatsumi Shrine (Iitate)|ja|山津見神社 (飯舘村)}} (Iitate, Fukushima Prefecture) * {{ill|Yamazu Shrine (Hamamatsu)|ja|山住神社}} (Hamamatsu, Shizuoka Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Sado)|ja|大山祇神社 (佐渡市)}} (Sado, Niigata Prefecture) - Former prefectural shrine; associated with the [[Sado Gold Mine]]. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)|ja|大山祇神社 (伊勢市)}} (Ise, Mie Prefecture) - A subsidiary shrine within the [[Ise Grand Shrines]][[Category:shrine disambiguations]] precincts. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nantan)|ja|大山祇神社 (南丹市)}} (Nantan, Kyoto Prefecture) - Not dedicated to Ōyamazumi. * {{ill|Bekkyū Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|別宮大山祇神社}} (Bekkyū, Imabari, Ehime Prefecture) - Former prefectural shrine. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社 (今治市大三島)|Ōyamazumi Shrine=Ōyamazumi Shrine|lt=Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ōmishima)}} - Head shrine of all Ōyamazumi shrines nationwide. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishi no Tani)|ja|大山祇神社 (西の谷)}} (Niihama, Ehime Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)|ja|大山積神社 (新居浜市)}} (Niihama, Ehime Prefecture) - Associated with the [[Besshi Copper Mine]]. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Dōdoigō)|ja|大山祇神社 (新上五島町道土井郷)}} (Dōdoigō, Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Imazatogō)|ja|大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)}} (Imazatogō, Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Saikai)|ja|大山祇神社 (西海市)}} (Saikai, Nagasaki Prefecture) - Associated with the [[Nishisonogi Coalfield]].
== Regional Distribution == === Hokkaido and Tohoku Region === ==== Hokkaido ==== * Ōyamazumi Shrine (Hakodate) * Ōyamazumi Shrine (Assabu)

==== Yamagata Prefecture ==== * Ōyamazumi Shrine (Kaminoyama) * Ōyamazumi Shrine (Sakata)

revision.text:
'''大山祇神社'''（おおやまづみじんじゃ）は、大山祇神（[[オオヤマツミ]]）を主祭神とする[[三島・大山祇信仰]]の[[神社]]。'''大山祗神社'''、'''大山積神社'''、'''大山津見神社'''、'''山祇神社'''、'''山積神社'''、'''山津見神社'''、'''山住神社'''、と書く神社もある。
1973年現在、北海道から鹿児島県まで全国に897社存在する。また、1995年の神社本庁の調査では293社、2019年の[[金沢工業大学]]の調査では960社存在するとされる。
総本社は、愛媛県今治市の[[大山祇神社]]である。他に、三島・大山祇信仰にもとづく神社名としては、[[三島神社]]などがある。
== 列挙 == 列挙している大山祇神社などは、以下のとおり。
* [[大山祇神社 (曖昧さ回避)#主な大山祇神社|大山祇神社]] * [[大山祇神社 (曖昧さ回避)#主な大山祇社|大山祇社]] * [[大山祇神社 (曖昧さ回避)#主な大山祗神社・大山積神社・大山津見神社|大山祗神社・大山積神社・大山津見神社]] * [[大山祇神社 (曖昧さ回避)#主な山祇神社|山祇神社]] * [[大山祇神社 (曖昧さ回避)#主な山積神社・山津見神社・山住神社|山積神社・山津見神社・山住神社]]

=== 関東地方 ===
* [[大山祇神社 (日立市大沼町)|大山祇神社]] （[[茨城県]][[日立市]]大沼町） * [[大山祇神社 (日立市助川町)|大山祇神社]] （茨城県日立市助川町） * [[大山祇神社 (足利市)|大山祇神社]] （[[栃木県]][[足利市]]大沼田町） * [[大山祇神社 (高崎市中室田町)|大山祇神社]] （[[群馬県]][[高崎市]][[中室田町]]） * [[大山祇神社 (高崎市箕郷町金敷平)|大山祇神社]] （群馬県高崎市箕郷町金敷平） * [[大山祇神社 (高崎市箕郷町矢原)|大山祇神社]] （群馬県高崎市箕郷町矢原） * [[大山祇神社 (太田市)|大山祇神社]] （群馬県[[太田市]][[山之神町]]） * [[大山祇神社 (沼田市)|大山祇神社]] （群馬県[[沼田市]]利根町） * [[大山祇神社 (館林市赤生田町)|大山祇神社]] （群馬県[[館林市]]赤生田町） * [[大山祇神社 (館林市高根町)|大山祇神社]] （群馬県館林市高根町） * [[大山祇神社 (渋川市赤城町)|大山祇神社]] （群馬県[[渋川市]]赤城町） * [[大山祇神社 (渋川市石原)|大山祇神社]] （群馬県渋川市石原） * [[大山祇神社 (渋川市横堀)|大山祇神社]] （群馬県渋川市横堀） * [[大山祇神社 (渋川市伊香保町)|大山祇神社]] （群馬県渋川市伊香保町） * [[大山祇神社 (みなかみ町)|大山祇神社]] （群馬県[[利根郡]][[みなかみ町]]相俣） * [[大山祇神社 (榛東村)|大山祇神社]] （群馬県[[北群馬郡]][[榛東村]]新井）
=== 中部地方 ===

==== 三重県 ====
* [[大山祇神社 (伊勢市)|大山祇神社]] （[[三重県]][[伊勢市]]宇治館町） – [[皇大神宮]]所管社 * [[大山祇神社 (鳥羽市)|大山祇神社]] （三重県[[鳥羽市]]鳥羽）
=== 近畿地方 === * [[大山祇神社 (京都市左京区)|大山祇神社]] （[[京都府]][[京都市]][[左京区]][[北白川]]） * [[大山祇神社 (京都市右京区)|大山祇神社]] （京都府京都市[[右京区]][[京北熊田町]]） * [[大山祇神社 (南丹市)|大山祇神社]] （[[京都府]][[南丹市]][[園部町大河内]]） – 祭神が大山祇神ではない
=== 四国地方 === * '''[[大山祇神社]]''' （[[愛媛県]][[今治市]]大三島町） – 総本社 * [[別宮大山祇神社]] （愛媛県今治市別宮町） – 旧県社 * [[大山祇神社 (西の谷)|大山祇神社]] （愛媛県[[新居浜市]]磯浦町）

* [[大山祗神社 (弘前市)|大山祗神社]] （青森県[[弘前市]][[水木在家]]） * 大山祗神社 （新潟県佐渡市相川） – [[大山祇神社 (佐渡市)|大山祇神社]]<ref>[https://niigata-jinjacho.jp 新潟神社庁]の表記は、大山祗神社。</ref>。旧県社。相川金銀山 ([[佐渡金山]]) 鎮護。 * [[大山祗神社 (佐渡市戸中)|大山祗神社]] （新潟県佐渡市戸中） * [[大山祗神社 (阿賀町豊実)|大山祗神社]] （新潟県東蒲原郡阿賀町豊実）

revision.text:
'''大山祇神社'''（おおやまづみじんじゃ）は、大山祇神（[[オオヤマツミ]]）を主祭神とする[[三島・大山祇信仰]]の[[神社]]。'''大山祗神社'''、'''大山積神社'''、'''大山津見神社'''、'''山祇神社'''、'''山積神社'''、'''山津見神社'''、'''山住神社'''、と書く神社もある。
1973年現在、北海道から鹿児島県まで全国に897社存在する。また、1995年の神社本庁の調査では293社、2019年の[[金沢工業大学]]の調査では960社存在するとされる。
総本社は、愛媛県今治市の[[大山祇神社]]である。他に、三島・大山祇信仰にもとづく神社名としては、[[三島神社]]などがある。
== 列挙 == 列挙している大山祇神社などは、以下のとおり。
* [[大山祇神社 (曖昧さ回避)#主な大山祇神社|大山祇神社]] * [[大山祇神社 (曖昧さ回避)#主な大山祇社|大山祇社]] * [[大山祇神社 (曖昧さ回避)#主な大山祗神社・大山積神社・大山津見神社|大山祗神社・大山積神社・大山津見神社]] * [[大山祇神社 (曖昧さ回避)#主な山祇神社|山祇神社]] * [[大山祇神社 (曖昧さ回避)#主な山積神社・山津見神社・山住神社|山積神社・山津見神社・山住神社]]

revision.text:
'''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社, Ōyamazumi Jinja)
Ōyamazumi Shrine is a [[Shinto shrines]][[Category:shrine disambiguations]] dedicated to {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|オオヤマツミ}}, associated with the [[Mishima and Ōyamazumi faith]]. As of 1973, 897 shrines existed across Japan, from [[Hokkaido]] to [[Kagoshima Prefecture]]. According to a 1995 survey by the Association of Shinto Shrines, there were 293 shrines, while a 2019 study by [[Kanazawa Institute of Technology]] reported 960 shrines. The shrine may also be written as '''Ōyamazumi Shrine''', '''Ōyamatsumi Shrine''', '''Yamazumi Shrine''', or '''Yamatsumi Shrine'''.
The head shrine is the {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社 (今治市大三島)|Ōyamazumi Shrine=Ōyamazumi Shrine|lt=Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ōmishima)}} in [[Ōmishima]], [[Imabari]], [[Ehime Prefecture]]. Other related shrine names under the [[Mishima and Ōyamazumi faith]] include {{ill|Mishima Shrine|ja|三島神社}}.
== Shrines with Articles == * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishiaizu)|ja|大山祇神社 (西会津町)}} (Nishiaizu, Fukushima Prefecture) * {{ill|Yamatsumi Shrine (Iitate)|ja|山津見神社 (飯舘村)}} (Iitate, Fukushima Prefecture) * {{ill|Yamazu Shrine (Hamamatsu)|ja|山住神社}} (Hamamatsu, Shizuoka Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Sado)|ja|大山祇神社 (佐渡市)}} (Sado, Niigata Prefecture) - Former prefectural shrine; associated with the [[Sado Gold Mine]]. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)|ja|大山祇神社 (伊勢市)}} (Ise, Mie Prefecture) - A subsidiary shrine within the [[Ise Grand Shrines]][[Category:shrine disambiguations]] precincts. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nantan)|ja|大山祇神社 (南丹市)}} (Nantan, Kyoto Prefecture) - Not dedicated to Ōyamazumi. * {{ill|Bekkyū Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|別宮大山祇神社}} (Bekkyū, Imabari, Ehime Prefecture) - Former prefectural shrine. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社 (今治市大三島)|Ōyamazumi Shrine=Ōyamazumi Shrine|lt=Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ōmishima)}} - Head shrine of all Ōyamazumi shrines nationwide. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishi no Tani)|ja|大山祇神社 (西の谷)}} (Niihama, Ehime Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)|ja|大山積神社 (新居浜市)}} (Niihama, Ehime Prefecture) - Associated with the [[Besshi Copper Mine]]. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Dōdoigō)|ja|大山祇神社 (新上五島町道土井郷)}} (Dōdoigō, Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Imazatogō)|ja|大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)}} (Imazatogō, Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Saikai)|ja|大山祇神社 (西海市)}} (Saikai, Nagasaki Prefecture) - Associated with the [[Nishisonogi Coalfield]].
== Regional Distribution == === Hokkaido and Tohoku Region === ==== Hokkaido ==== * Ōyamazumi Shrine (Hakodate) * Ōyamazumi Shrine (Assabu)

revision.comment: Moving from [[Category:Mishima and Ōyamazumi faith]] to [[Category:Pages to be overwritten]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''大山祇神社'''（おおやまづみじんじゃ）は、大山祇神（[[オオヤマツミ]]）を主祭神とする[[三島・大山祇信仰]]の[[神社]]。'''大山祗神社'''、'''大山積神社'''、'''大山津見神社'''、'''山祇神社'''、'''山積神社'''、'''山津見神社'''、'''山住神社'''、と書く神社もある。
1973年現在、北海道から鹿児島県まで全国に897社存在する。また、1995年の神社本庁の調査では293社、2019年の[[金沢工業大学]]の調査では960社存在するとされる。
総本社は、愛媛県今治市の[[大山祇神社]]である。他に、三島・大山祇信仰にもとづく神社名としては、[[三島神社]]などがある。
== 列挙 == 列挙している大山祇神社などは、以下のとおり。
* [[大山祇神社 (曖昧さ回避)#主な大山祇神社|大山祇神社]] * [[大山祇神社 (曖昧さ回避)#主な大山祇社|大山祇社]] * [[大山祇神社 (曖昧さ回避)#主な大山祗神社・大山積神社・大山津見神社|大山祗神社・大山積神社・大山津見神社]] * [[大山祇神社 (曖昧さ回避)#主な山祇神社|山祇神社]] * [[大山祇神社 (曖昧さ回避)#主な山積神社・山津見神社・山住神社|山積神社・山津見神社・山住神社]]

=== 近畿地方 === * [[大山祇神社 (京都市左京区)|大山祇神社]] （[[京都府]][[京都市]][[左京区]][[北白川]]） * [[大山祇神社 (京都市右京区)|大山祇神社]] （京都府京都市[[右京区]][[京北熊田町]]） * [[大山祇神社 (南丹市)|大山祇神社]] （[[京都府]][[南丹市]][[園部町大河内]]） – 祭神が大山祇神ではない

=== 九州地方 === * [[大山祇神社 (飯塚市桑曲)|大山祇神社]] （[[福岡県]][[飯塚市]]桑曲） * [[大山祇神社 (飯塚市内住)|大山祇神社]] （福岡県飯塚市内住） * [[大山祇神社 (宇美町)|大山祇神社]] （福岡県[[糟屋郡]][[宇美町]]炭焼）

== 主な大山祇社 ==

* [[大山祗神社 (弘前市)|大山祗神社]] （青森県[[弘前市]][[水木在家]]） * 大山祗神社 （新潟県佐渡市相川） – [[大山祇神社 (佐渡市)|大山祇神社]]<ref>[https://niigata-jinjacho.jp 新潟神社庁]の表記は、大山祗神社。</ref>。旧県社。相川金銀山 ([[佐渡金山]]) 鎮護。 * [[大山祗神社 (佐渡市戸中)|大山祗神社]] （新潟県佐渡市戸中） * [[大山祗神社 (阿賀町豊実)|大山祗神社]] （新潟県東蒲原郡阿賀町豊実）

=== 大山津見神社 ===

revision.comment: Text replacement - "qq" to "11"
revision.text:
'''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社, Ōyamazumi Jinja)
Ōyamazumi Shrine is a [[Shinto shrines]][[Category:shrine disambiguations]] dedicated to {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|オオヤマツミ}}, associated with the [[Mishima and Ōyamazumi faith]]. As of 1973, 897 shrines existed across Japan, from [[Hokkaido]] to [[Kagoshima Prefecture]]. According to a 1995 survey by the Association of Shinto Shrines, there were 293 shrines, while a 2019 study by [[Kanazawa Institute of Technology]] reported 960 shrines. The shrine may also be written as '''Ōyamazumi Shrine''', '''Ōyamatsumi Shrine''', '''Yamazumi Shrine''', or '''Yamatsumi Shrine'''.
The head shrine is the {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社 (今治市大三島)|Ōyamazumi Shrine=Ōyamazumi Shrine|lt=Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ōmishima)}} in [[Ōmishima]], [[Imabari]], [[Ehime Prefecture]]. Other related shrine names under the [[Mishima and Ōyamazumi faith]] include {{ill|Mishima Shrine|ja|三島神社}}.
== Shrines with Articles == * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishiaizu)|ja|大山祇神社 (西会津町)}} (Nishiaizu, Fukushima Prefecture) * {{ill|Yamatsumi Shrine (Iitate)|ja|山津見神社 (飯舘村)}} (Iitate, Fukushima Prefecture) * {{ill|Yamazu Shrine (Hamamatsu)|ja|山住神社}} (Hamamatsu, Shizuoka Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Sado)|ja|大山祇神社 (佐渡市)}} (Sado, Niigata Prefecture) - Former prefectural shrine; associated with the [[Sado Gold Mine]]. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)|ja|大山祇神社 (伊勢市)}} (Ise, Mie Prefecture) - A subsidiary shrine within the [[Ise Grand Shrines]][[Category:shrine disambiguations]] precincts. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nantan)|ja|大山祇神社 (南丹市)}} (Nantan, Kyoto Prefecture) - Not dedicated to Ōyamazumi. * {{ill|Bekkyū Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|別宮大山祇神社}} (Bekkyū, Imabari, Ehime Prefecture) - Former prefectural shrine. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社 (今治市大三島)|Ōyamazumi Shrine=Ōyamazumi Shrine|lt=Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ōmishima)}} - Head shrine of all Ōyamazumi shrines nationwide. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishi no Tani)|ja|大山祇神社 (西の谷)}} (Niihama, Ehime Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)|ja|大山積神社 (新居浜市)}} (Niihama, Ehime Prefecture) - Associated with the [[Besshi Copper Mine]]. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Dōdoigō)|ja|大山祇神社 (新上五島町道土井郷)}} (Dōdoigō, Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Imazatogō)|ja|大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)}} (Imazatogō, Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Saikai)|ja|大山祇神社 (西海市)}} (Saikai, Nagasaki Prefecture) - Associated with the [[Nishisonogi Coalfield]].
== Regional Distribution == === Hokkaido and Tohoku Region === ==== Hokkaido ==== * Ōyamazumi Shrine (Hakodate) * Ōyamazumi Shrine (Assabu)
==== Aomori Prefecture ==== * Ōyamazumi Shrine (Aomori City) * Yamazumi Shrine (Hiranai) * Ōyamazumi Shrine (Sōma Village) * Ōyamazumi Shrine (Goshogawara) * Ōyamazumi Shrine (Kawauchi) * Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ōhata, Komemura) * Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ōhata, Takahashigawa) * Ōyamazumi Shrine (Wakinosawa) * Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ajigasawa) * Ōyamazumi Shrine (Kizukuri) * Ōyamazumi Shrine (Shariki) * Ōyamazumi Shrine (Kuroishi) * Yamazumi Shrine (Kuroishi)
==== Akita Prefecture ==== * Ōyamazumi Shrine (Kamikoani) * Ōyamazumi Shrine (Yurihonjō) * Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nikaho) * Ōyamazumi Shrine (Daisen)
==== Iwate Prefecture ==== * Yamazumi Shrine (Shizukuishi, Minamihata) * Yamazumi Shrine (Shizukuishi, Omaegami-domae) * Yamazumi Shrine (Shizukuishi, Kosaka) * Yamazumi Shrine (Shizukuishi, Nishine) * Yamazumi Shrine (Morioka, Sunagosawa) * Yamazumi Shrine (Morioka, Kuroishino) * Yamazumi Shrine (Shiwa, Katayose) * Yamazumi Shrine (Shiwa, Yamaya) * Yamazumi Shrine (Nishiwaga, Sawauchi, Kawafune) * Yamazumi Shrine (Nishiwaga, Sawauchi, Kaisawa) * Yamazumi Shrine (Nishiwaga, Yunosawa) * Yamazumi Shrine (Nishiwaga, Konagase) * Yamazumi Shrine (Nishiwaga, Yanagisawa) * Yamazumi Shrine (Nishiwaga, Mimizu) * Yamazumi Shrine (Nishiwaga, Sawauchi) * Yamazumi Shrine (Nishiwaga, Sasō) * Yamazumi Shrine (Nishiwaga, Shitamae) * Yamazumi Shrine (Hanamaki, Ishidōya) * Yamazumi Shrine (Hanamaki, Shitashizawa) * Yamazumi Shrine (Hanamaki, Namari) * Yamazumi Shrine (Hanamaki, Matsuzono) * Yamazumi Shrine (Hanamaki, Shirihirakawa) * Yamazu Shrine (Kamaishi) * Yamazumi Shrine (Kitakami, Hato-oka) * Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ōshū)
==== Yamagata Prefecture ==== * Ōyamazumi Shrine (Kaminoyama) * Ōyamazumi Shrine (Sakata)
==== Miyagi Prefecture ==== * Ōyamazumi Shrine (Sendai) * Yamazumi Shrine (Higashimatsuyama) * Yamazumi Shrine (Ishinomaki)
==== Fukushima Prefecture ==== * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishiaizu)|ja|大山祇神社 (西会津町)}} * {{ill|Yamatsumi Shrine (Iitate)|ja|山津見神社 (飯舘村)}} * Ōyamazumi Shrine (Kōriyama)

revision.text:
'''大山祇神社'''（おおやまづみじんじゃ）は、大山祇神（[[オオヤマツミ]]）を主祭神とする[[三島・大山祇信仰]]の[[神社]]。'''大山祗神社'''、'''大山積神社'''、'''大山津見神社'''、'''山祇神社'''、'''山積神社'''、'''山津見神社'''、'''山住神社'''、と書く神社もある。
1973年現在、北海道から鹿児島県まで全国に897社存在する。また、1995年の神社本庁の調査では293社、2019年の[[金沢工業大学]]の調査では960社存在するとされる。
総本社は、愛媛県今治市の[[大山祇神社]]である。他に、三島・大山祇信仰にもとづく神社名としては、[[三島神社]]などがある。
== 列挙 == 列挙している大山祇神社などは、以下のとおり。
* [[大山祇神社 (曖昧さ回避)#主な大山祇神社|大山祇神社]] * [[大山祇神社 (曖昧さ回避)#主な大山祇社|大山祇社]] * [[大山祇神社 (曖昧さ回避)#主な大山祗神社・大山積神社・大山津見神社|大山祗神社・大山積神社・大山津見神社]] * [[大山祇神社 (曖昧さ回避)#主な山祇神社|山祇神社]] * [[大山祇神社 (曖昧さ回避)#主な山積神社・山津見神社・山住神社|山積神社・山津見神社・山住神社]]
== 主な大山祇神社 ==
=== 北海道地方 === * [[大山祇神社 (函館市)|大山祇神社]] （[[北海道]][[函館市]]庵原町） * [[大山祇神社 (厚沢部町)|大山祇神社]] （北海道[[檜山郡]][[厚沢部町]]当路）

'''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社, Ōyamazumi Jinja)
Ōyamazumi Shrine is a [[Shinto shrines]][[Category:shrine disambiguations]] dedicated to {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|オオヤマツミ}}, associated with the [[Mishima and Ōyamazumi faith]]. As of 1973, 897 shrines existed across Japan, from [[Hokkaido]] to [[Kagoshima Prefecture]]. According to a 1995 survey by the Association of Shinto Shrines, there were 293 shrines, while a 2019 study by [[Kanazawa Institute of Technology]] reported 960 shrines. The shrine may also be written as '''Ōyamazumi Shrine''', '''Ōyamatsumi Shrine''', '''Yamazumi Shrine''', or '''Yamatsumi Shrine'''.
The head shrine is the {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社 (今治市大三島)|Ōyamazumi Shrine=Ōyamazumi Shrine|lt=Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ōmishima)}} in [[Ōmishima]], [[Imabari]], [[Ehime Prefecture]]. Other related shrine names under the [[Mishima and Ōyamazumi faith]] include {{ill|Mishima Shrine|ja|三島神社}}.
== Shrines with Articles == * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishiaizu)|ja|大山祇神社 (西会津町)}} (Nishiaizu, Fukushima Prefecture) * {{ill|Yamatsumi Shrine (Iitate)|ja|山津見神社 (飯舘村)}} (Iitate, Fukushima Prefecture) * {{ill|Yamazu Shrine (Hamamatsu)|ja|山住神社}} (Hamamatsu, Shizuoka Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Sado)|ja|大山祇神社 (佐渡市)}} (Sado, Niigata Prefecture) - Former prefectural shrine; associated with the [[Sado Gold Mine]]. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)|ja|大山祇神社 (伊勢市)}} (Ise, Mie Prefecture) - A subsidiary shrine within the [[Ise Grand Shrines]][[Category:shrine disambiguations]] precincts. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nantan)|ja|大山祇神社 (南丹市)}} (Nantan, Kyoto Prefecture) - Not dedicated to Ōyamazumi. * {{ill|Bekkyū Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|別宮大山祇神社}} (Bekkyū, Imabari, Ehime Prefecture) - Former prefectural shrine. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社 (今治市大三島)|Ōyamazumi Shrine=Ōyamazumi Shrine|lt=Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ōmishima)}} - Head shrine of all Ōyamazumi shrines nationwide. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishi no Tani)|ja|大山祇神社 (西の谷)}} (Niihama, Ehime Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)|ja|大山積神社 (新居浜市)}} (Niihama, Ehime Prefecture) - Associated with the [[Besshi Copper Mine]]. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Dōdoigō)|ja|大山祇神社 (新上五島町道土井郷)}} (Dōdoigō, Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Imazatogō)|ja|大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)}} (Imazatogō, Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Saikai)|ja|大山祇神社 (西海市)}} (Saikai, Nagasaki Prefecture) - Associated with the [[Nishisonogi Coalfield]].
== Regional Distribution == === Hokkaido and Tohoku Region === ==== Hokkaido ==== * Ōyamazumi Shrine (Hakodate) * Ōyamazumi Shrine (Assabu)

revision.comment: Text replacement - "|大山祇神社 (西会津町)" to "|大山祇神社 (西会津町)|1=Oyamatsumi Shrine (Nishi Aizu)"
revision.text:
'''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社, Ōyamazumi Jinja)
Ōyamazumi Shrine is a [[Shinto shrines]][[Category:shrine disambiguations]] dedicated to {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|オオヤマツミ|1=Ōyamatsumi}}, associated with the [[Mishima and Ōyamazumi faith]]. As of 1973, 897 shrines existed across Japan, from [[Hokkaido]] to [[Kagoshima Prefecture]]. According to a 1995 survey by the Association of Shinto Shrines, there were 293 shrines, while a 2019 study by [[Kanazawa Institute of Technology]] reported 960 shrines. The shrine may also be written as '''Ōyamazumi Shrine''', '''Ōyamatsumi Shrine''', '''Yamazumi Shrine''', or '''Yamatsumi Shrine'''.
The head shrine is the {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社 (今治市大三島)|Ōyamazumi Shrine=Ōyamazumi Shrine|lt=Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ōmishima)}} in [[Ōmishima]], [[Imabari]], [[Ehime Prefecture]]. Other related shrine names under the [[Mishima and Ōyamazumi faith]] include {{ill|Mishima Shrine|ja|三島神社}}.
== Shrines with Articles == * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishiaizu)|ja|大山祇神社 (西会津町)|1=Oyamatsumi Shrine (Nishi Aizu)}} (Nishiaizu, Fukushima Prefecture) * {{ill|Yamatsumi Shrine (Iitate)|ja|山津見神社 (飯舘村)}} (Iitate, Fukushima Prefecture) * {{ill|Yamazu Shrine (Hamamatsu)|ja|山住神社}} (Hamamatsu, Shizuoka Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Sado)|ja|大山祇神社 (佐渡市)}} (Sado, Niigata Prefecture) - Former prefectural shrine; associated with the [[Sado Gold Mine]]. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)|ja|大山祇神社 (伊勢市)}} (Ise, Mie Prefecture) - A subsidiary shrine within the [[Ise Grand Shrines]][[Category:shrine disambiguations]] precincts. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nantan)|ja|大山祇神社 (南丹市)}} (Nantan, Kyoto Prefecture) - Not dedicated to Ōyamazumi. * {{ill|Bekkyū Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|別宮大山祇神社}} (Bekkyū, Imabari, Ehime Prefecture) - Former prefectural shrine. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社 (今治市大三島)|Ōyamazumi Shrine=Ōyamazumi Shrine|lt=Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ōmishima)}} - Head shrine of all Ōyamazumi shrines nationwide. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishi no Tani)|ja|大山祇神社 (西の谷)}} (Niihama, Ehime Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)|ja|大山積神社 (新居浜市)}} (Niihama, Ehime Prefecture) - Associated with the [[Besshi Copper Mine]]. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Dōdoigō)|ja|大山祇神社 (新上五島町道土井郷)}} (Dōdoigō, Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Imazatogō)|ja|大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)}} (Imazatogō, Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Saikai)|ja|大山祇神社 (西海市)}} (Saikai, Nagasaki Prefecture) - Associated with the [[Nishisonogi Coalfield]].

revision.text:
'''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社, Ōyamazumi Jinja)
Ōyamazumi Shrine is a [[Shinto shrines]][[Category:shrine disambiguations]] dedicated to {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|オオヤマツミ|1=Ōyamatsumi}}, associated with the [[Mishima and Ōyamazumi faith]]. As of 1973, 897 shrines existed across Japan, from [[Hokkaido]] to [[Kagoshima Prefecture]]. According to a 1995 survey by the Association of Shinto Shrines, there were 293 shrines, while a 2019 study by [[Kanazawa Institute of Technology]] reported 960 shrines. The shrine may also be written as '''Ōyamazumi Shrine''', '''Ōyamatsumi Shrine''', '''Yamazumi Shrine''', or '''Yamatsumi Shrine'''.
The head shrine is the {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社 (今治市大三島)|Ōyamazumi Shrine=Ōyamazumi Shrine|lt=Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ōmishima)}} in [[Ōmishima]], [[Imabari]], [[Ehime Prefecture]]. Other related shrine names under the [[Mishima and Ōyamazumi faith]] include {{ill|Mishima Shrine|ja|三島神社}}.
== Shrines with Articles == * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishiaizu)|ja|大山祇神社 (西会津町)|1=Oyamatsumi Shrine (Nishi Aizu)}} (Nishiaizu, Fukushima Prefecture) * {{ill|Yamatsumi Shrine (Iitate)|ja|山津見神社 (飯舘村)}} (Iitate, Fukushima Prefecture) * {{ill|Yamazu Shrine (Hamamatsu)|ja|山住神社}} (Hamamatsu, Shizuoka Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Sado)|ja|大山祇神社 (佐渡市)}} (Sado, Niigata Prefecture) - Former prefectural shrine; associated with the [[Sado Gold Mine]]. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)|ja|大山祇神社 (伊勢市)}} (Ise, Mie Prefecture) - A subsidiary shrine within the [[Ise Grand Shrines]][[Category:shrine disambiguations]] precincts. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nantan)|ja|大山祇神社 (南丹市)}} (Nantan, Kyoto Prefecture) - Not dedicated to Ōyamazumi. * {{ill|Bekkyū Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|別宮大山祇神社|1=Betsugu Oyamatsumi Shrine}} (Bekkyū, Imabari, Ehime Prefecture) - Former prefectural shrine. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社 (今治市大三島)|Ōyamazumi Shrine=Ōyamazumi Shrine|lt=Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ōmishima)}} - Head shrine of all Ōyamazumi shrines nationwide. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishi no Tani)|ja|大山祇神社 (西の谷)}} (Niihama, Ehime Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)|ja|大山積神社 (新居浜市)}} (Niihama, Ehime Prefecture) - Associated with the [[Besshi Copper Mine]]. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Dōdoigō)|ja|大山祇神社 (新上五島町道土井郷)}} (Dōdoigō, Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Imazatogō)|ja|大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)}} (Imazatogō, Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Saikai)|ja|大山祇神社 (西海市)}} (Saikai, Nagasaki Prefecture) - Associated with the [[Nishisonogi Coalfield]].
== Regional Distribution == === Hokkaido and Tohoku Region === ==== Hokkaido ==== * Ōyamazumi Shrine (Hakodate) * Ōyamazumi Shrine (Assabu)

revision.text:
'''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社, Ōyamazumi Jinja)
Ōyamazumi Shrine is a [[Shinto shrines]][[Category:shrine disambiguations]] dedicated to {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|オオヤマツミ|1=Ōyamatsumi}}, associated with the [[Mishima and Ōyamazumi faith]]. As of 1973, 897 shrines existed across Japan, from [[Hokkaido]] to [[Kagoshima Prefecture]]. According to a 1995 survey by the Association of Shinto Shrines, there were 293 shrines, while a 2019 study by [[Kanazawa Institute of Technology]] reported 960 shrines. The shrine may also be written as '''Ōyamazumi Shrine''', '''Ōyamatsumi Shrine''', '''Yamazumi Shrine''', or '''Yamatsumi Shrine'''.
The head shrine is the {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社 (今治市大三島)|Ōyamazumi Shrine=Ōyamazumi Shrine|lt=Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ōmishima)}} in [[Ōmishima]], [[Imabari]], [[Ehime Prefecture]]. Other related shrine names under the [[Mishima and Ōyamazumi faith]] include {{ill|Mishima Shrine|ja|三島神社}}.
== Shrines with Articles == * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishiaizu)|ja|大山祇神社 (西会津町)|1=Oyamatsumi Shrine (Nishi Aizu)}} (Nishiaizu, Fukushima Prefecture) * {{ill|Yamatsumi Shrine (Iitate)|ja|山津見神社 (飯舘村)}} (Iitate, Fukushima Prefecture) * {{ill|Yamazu Shrine (Hamamatsu)|ja|山住神社}} (Hamamatsu, Shizuoka Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Sado)|ja|大山祇神社 (佐渡市)}} (Sado, Niigata Prefecture) - Former prefectural shrine; associated with the [[Sado Gold Mine]]. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)|ja|大山祇神社 (伊勢市)}} (Ise, Mie Prefecture) - A subsidiary shrine within the [[Ise Grand Shrines]][[Category:shrine disambiguations]] precincts. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nantan)|ja|大山祇神社 (南丹市)}} (Nantan, Kyoto Prefecture) - Not dedicated to Ōyamazumi. * {{ill|Bekkyū Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|別宮大山祇神社|1=Betsugu Oyamatsumi Shrine}} (Bekkyū, Imabari, Ehime Prefecture) - Former prefectural shrine. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社 (今治市大三島)|Ōyamazumi Shrine=Ōyamazumi Shrine|lt=Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ōmishima)}} - Head shrine of all Ōyamazumi shrines nationwide. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishi no Tani)|ja|大山祇神社 (西の谷)}} (Niihama, Ehime Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)|ja|大山積神社 (新居浜市)}} (Niihama, Ehime Prefecture) - Associated with the [[Besshi Copper Mine]]. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Dōdoigō)|ja|大山祇神社 (新上五島町道土井郷)}} (Dōdoigō, Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Imazatogō)|ja|大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)}} (Imazatogō, Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Saikai)|ja|大山祇神社 (西海市)}} (Saikai, Nagasaki Prefecture) - Associated with the [[Nishisonogi Coalfield]].
== Regional Distribution == === Hokkaido and Tohoku Region === ==== Hokkaido ==== * Ōyamazumi Shrine (Hakodate) * Ōyamazumi Shrine (Assabu)
==== Aomori Prefecture ==== * Ōyamazumi Shrine (Aomori City) * Yamazumi Shrine (Hiranai) * Ōyamazumi Shrine (Sōma Village) * Ōyamazumi Shrine (Goshogawara) * Ōyamazumi Shrine (Kawauchi) * Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ōhata, Komemura) * Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ōhata, Takahashigawa) * Ōyamazumi Shrine (Wakinosawa) * Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ajigasawa) * Ōyamazumi Shrine (Kizukuri) * Ōyamazumi Shrine (Shariki) * Ōyamazumi Shrine (Kuroishi) * Yamazumi Shrine (Kuroishi)
==== Akita Prefecture ==== * Ōyamazumi Shrine (Kamikoani) * Ōyamazumi Shrine (Yurihonjō) * Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nikaho) * Ōyamazumi Shrine (Daisen)
==== Iwate Prefecture ==== * Yamazumi Shrine (Shizukuishi, Minamihata) * Yamazumi Shrine (Shizukuishi, Omaegami-domae) * Yamazumi Shrine (Shizukuishi, Kosaka) * Yamazumi Shrine (Shizukuishi, Nishine) * Yamazumi Shrine (Morioka, Sunagosawa) * Yamazumi Shrine (Morioka, Kuroishino) * Yamazumi Shrine (Shiwa, Katayose) * Yamazumi Shrine (Shiwa, Yamaya) * Yamazumi Shrine (Nishiwaga, Sawauchi, Kawafune) * Yamazumi Shrine (Nishiwaga, Sawauchi, Kaisawa) * Yamazumi Shrine (Nishiwaga, Yunosawa) * Yamazumi Shrine (Nishiwaga, Konagase) * Yamazumi Shrine (Nishiwaga, Yanagisawa) * Yamazumi Shrine (Nishiwaga, Mimizu) * Yamazumi Shrine (Nishiwaga, Sawauchi) * Yamazumi Shrine (Nishiwaga, Sasō) * Yamazumi Shrine (Nishiwaga, Shitamae) * Yamazumi Shrine (Hanamaki, Ishidōya) * Yamazumi Shrine (Hanamaki, Shitashizawa) * Yamazumi Shrine (Hanamaki, Namari) * Yamazumi Shrine (Hanamaki, Matsuzono) * Yamazumi Shrine (Hanamaki, Shirihirakawa) * Yamazu Shrine (Kamaishi) * Yamazumi Shrine (Kitakami, Hato-oka) * Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ōshū)
==== Yamagata Prefecture ==== * Ōyamazumi Shrine (Kaminoyama) * Ōyamazumi Shrine (Sakata)

revision.text:
'''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社, Ōyamazumi Jinja)
Ōyamazumi Shrine is a [[Shinto shrines]][[Category:shrine disambiguations]] dedicated to {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|オオヤマツミ|1=Ōyamatsumi}}, associated with the [[Mishima and Ōyamazumi faith]]. As of 1973, 897 shrines existed across Japan, from [[Hokkaido]] to [[Kagoshima Prefecture]]. According to a 1995 survey by the Association of Shinto Shrines, there were 293 shrines, while a 2019 study by [[Kanazawa Institute of Technology]] reported 960 shrines. The shrine may also be written as '''Ōyamazumi Shrine''', '''Ōyamatsumi Shrine''', '''Yamazumi Shrine''', or '''Yamatsumi Shrine'''.
The head shrine is the {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社 (今治市大三島)|Ōyamazumi Shrine=Ōyamazumi Shrine|lt=Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ōmishima)}} in [[Ōmishima]], [[Imabari]], [[Ehime Prefecture]]. Other related shrine names under the [[Mishima and Ōyamazumi faith]] include {{ill|Mishima Shrine|ja|三島神社}}.
== Shrines with Articles == * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishiaizu)|ja|大山祇神社 (西会津町)|1=Oyamatsumi Shrine (Nishi Aizu)}} (Nishiaizu, Fukushima Prefecture) * {{ill|Yamatsumi Shrine (Iitate)|ja|山津見神社 (飯舘村)}} (Iitate, Fukushima Prefecture) * {{ill|Yamazu Shrine (Hamamatsu)|ja|山住神社}} (Hamamatsu, Shizuoka Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Sado)|ja|大山祇神社 (佐渡市)}} (Sado, Niigata Prefecture) - Former prefectural shrine; associated with the [[Sado Gold Mine]]. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)|ja|大山祇神社 (伊勢市)}} (Ise, Mie Prefecture) - A subsidiary shrine within the [[Ise Grand Shrines]][[Category:shrine disambiguations]] precincts. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nantan)|ja|大山祇神社 (南丹市)}} (Nantan, Kyoto Prefecture) - Not dedicated to Ōyamazumi. * {{ill|Bekkyū Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|別宮大山祇神社|1=Betsugu Oyamatsumi Shrine}} (Bekkyū, Imabari, Ehime Prefecture) - Former prefectural shrine. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社 (今治市大三島)|Ōyamazumi Shrine=Ōyamazumi Shrine|lt=Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ōmishima)}} - Head shrine of all Ōyamazumi shrines nationwide. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishi no Tani)|ja|大山祇神社 (西の谷)}} (Niihama, Ehime Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)|ja|大山積神社 (新居浜市)}} (Niihama, Ehime Prefecture) - Associated with the [[Besshi Copper Mine]]. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Dōdoigō)|ja|大山祇神社 (新上五島町道土井郷)}} (Dōdoigō, Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Imazatogō)|ja|大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)}} (Imazatogō, Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Saikai)|ja|大山祇神社 (西海市)}} (Saikai, Nagasaki Prefecture) - Associated with the [[Nishisonogi Coalfield]].
== Regional Distribution == === Hokkaido and Tohoku Region === ==== Hokkaido ==== * Ōyamazumi Shrine (Hakodate) * Ōyamazumi Shrine (Assabu)

revision.text:
'''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社, Ōyamazumi Jinja)
Ōyamazumi Shrine is a [[Shinto shrines]] dedicated to {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|オオヤマツミ|1=Ōyamatsumi|12=draft}}, associated with the [[Mishima and Ōyamazumi faith]]. As of 1973, 897 shrines existed across Japan, from [[Hokkaido]] to [[Kagoshima Prefecture]]. According to a 1995 survey by the Association of Shinto Shrines, there were 293 shrines, while a 2019 study by [[Kanazawa Institute of Technology]] reported 960 shrines. The shrine may also be written as '''Ōyamazumi Shrine''', '''Ōyamatsumi Shrine''', '''Yamazumi Shrine''', or '''Yamatsumi Shrine'''.
The head shrine is the {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社 (今治市大三島)|Ōyamazumi Shrine=Ōyamazumi Shrine|lt=Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ōmishima)|12=draft}} in [[Ōmishima]], [[Imabari]], [[Ehime Prefecture]]. Other related shrine names under the [[Mishima and Ōyamazumi faith]] include {{ill|Mishima Shrine|ja|三島神社|12=draft}}.
== Shrines with Articles == * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishiaizu)|ja|大山祇神社 (西会津町)|1=Oyamatsumi Shrine (Nishi Aizu)|12=draft}} (Nishiaizu, Fukushima Prefecture) * {{ill|Yamatsumi Shrine (Iitate)|ja|山津見神社 (飯舘村)|12=draft}} (Iitate, Fukushima Prefecture) * {{ill|Yamazu Shrine (Hamamatsu)|ja|山住神社|12=draft}} (Hamamatsu, Shizuoka Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Sado)|ja|大山祇神社 (佐渡市)|12=draft}} (Sado, Niigata Prefecture) - Former prefectural shrine; associated with the [[Sado Gold Mine]]. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)|ja|大山祇神社 (伊勢市)|12=draft}} (Ise, Mie Prefecture) - A subsidiary shrine within the [[Ise Grand Shrines]] precincts. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nantan)|ja|大山祇神社 (南丹市)|12=draft}} (Nantan, Kyoto Prefecture) - Not dedicated to Ōyamazumi. * {{ill|Bekkyū Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|別宮大山祇神社|1=Betsugu Oyamatsumi Shrine|12=draft}} (Bekkyū, Imabari, Ehime Prefecture) - Former prefectural shrine. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社 (今治市大三島)|Ōyamazumi Shrine=Ōyamazumi Shrine|lt=Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ōmishima)|12=draft}} - Head shrine of all Ōyamazumi shrines nationwide. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishi no Tani)|ja|大山祇神社 (西の谷)|12=draft}} (Niihama, Ehime Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)|ja|大山積神社 (新居浜市)|12=draft}} (Niihama, Ehime Prefecture) - Associated with the [[Besshi Copper Mine]]. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Dōdoigō)|ja|大山祇神社 (新上五島町道土井郷)|12=draft}} (Dōdoigō, Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Imazatogō)|ja|大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)|12=draft}} (Imazatogō, Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Saikai)|ja|大山祇神社 (西海市)|12=draft}} (Saikai, Nagasaki Prefecture) - Associated with the [[Nishisonogi Coalfield]].

'''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社, Ōyamazumi Jinja)
Ōyamazumi Shrine is a [[Shinto shrines]] dedicated to {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|オオヤマツミ|1=Ōyamatsumi|12=draft}}, associated with the [[Mishima and Ōyamazumi faith]]. As of 1973, 897 shrines existed across Japan, from [[Hokkaido]] to [[Kagoshima Prefecture]]. According to a 1995 survey by the Association of Shinto Shrines, there were 293 shrines, while a 2019 study by [[Kanazawa Institute of Technology]] reported 960 shrines. The shrine may also be written as '''Ōyamazumi Shrine''', '''Ōyamatsumi Shrine''', '''Yamazumi Shrine''', or '''Yamatsumi Shrine'''.
The head shrine is the {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社 (今治市大三島)|Ōyamazumi Shrine=Ōyamazumi Shrine|lt=Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ōmishima)|12=draft}} in [[Ōmishima]], [[Imabari]], [[Ehime Prefecture]]. Other related shrine names under the [[Mishima and Ōyamazumi faith]] include {{ill|Mishima Shrine|ja|三島神社|12=draft}}.
== Shrines with Articles == * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishiaizu)|ja|大山祇神社 (西会津町)|1=Oyamatsumi Shrine (Nishi Aizu)|12=draft}} (Nishiaizu, Fukushima Prefecture) * {{ill|Yamatsumi Shrine (Iitate)|ja|山津見神社 (飯舘村)|12=draft}} (Iitate, Fukushima Prefecture) * {{ill|Yamazu Shrine (Hamamatsu)|ja|山住神社|12=draft}} (Hamamatsu, Shizuoka Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Sado)|ja|大山祇神社 (佐渡市)|12=draft}} (Sado, Niigata Prefecture) - Former prefectural shrine; associated with the [[Sado Gold Mine]]. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)|ja|大山祇神社 (伊勢市)|12=draft}} (Ise, Mie Prefecture) - A subsidiary shrine within the [[Ise Grand Shrines]] precincts. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nantan)|ja|大山祇神社 (南丹市)|12=draft}} (Nantan, Kyoto Prefecture) - Not dedicated to Ōyamazumi. * {{ill|Bekkyū Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|別宮大山祇神社|1=Betsugu Oyamatsumi Shrine|12=draft}} (Bekkyū, Imabari, Ehime Prefecture) - Former prefectural shrine. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社 (今治市大三島)|Ōyamazumi Shrine=Ōyamazumi Shrine|lt=Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ōmishima)|12=draft}} - Head shrine of all Ōyamazumi shrines nationwide. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishi no Tani)|ja|大山祇神社 (西の谷)|12=draft}} (Niihama, Ehime Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)|ja|大山積神社 (新居浜市)|12=draft}} (Niihama, Ehime Prefecture) - Associated with the [[Besshi Copper Mine]]. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Dōdoigō)|ja|大山祇神社 (新上五島町道土井郷)|12=draft}} (Dōdoigō, Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Imazatogō)|ja|大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)|12=draft}} (Imazatogō, Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Saikai)|ja|大山祇神社 (西海市)|12=draft}} (Saikai, Nagasaki Prefecture) - Associated with the [[Nishisonogi Coalfield]].

'''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社, Ōyamazumi Jinja)
Ōyamazumi Shrine is a [[Shinto shrines]] dedicated to {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|オオヤマツミ|1=Ōyamatsumi|12=draft}}, associated with the [[Mishima and Ōyamazumi faith]]. As of 1973, 897 shrines existed across Japan, from [[Hokkaido]] to [[Kagoshima Prefecture]]. According to a 1995 survey by the Association of Shinto Shrines, there were 293 shrines, while a 2019 study by [[Kanazawa Institute of Technology]] reported 960 shrines. The shrine may also be written as '''Ōyamazumi Shrine''', '''Ōyamatsumi Shrine''', '''Yamazumi Shrine''', or '''Yamatsumi Shrine'''.
The head shrine is the {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社 (今治市大三島)|Ōyamazumi Shrine=Ōyamazumi Shrine|lt=Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ōmishima)|12=draft}} in [[Ōmishima]], [[Imabari]], [[Ehime Prefecture]]. Other related shrine names under the [[Mishima and Ōyamazumi faith]] include {{ill|Mishima Shrine|ja|三島神社|12=draft}}.
== Shrines with Articles == * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishiaizu)|ja|大山祇神社 (西会津町)|1=Oyamatsumi Shrine (Nishi Aizu)|12=draft}} (Nishiaizu, Fukushima Prefecture) * {{ill|Yamatsumi Shrine (Iitate)|ja|山津見神社 (飯舘村)|12=draft}} (Iitate, Fukushima Prefecture) * {{ill|Yamazu Shrine (Hamamatsu)|ja|山住神社|12=draft}} (Hamamatsu, Shizuoka Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Sado)|ja|大山祇神社 (佐渡市)|12=draft}} (Sado, Niigata Prefecture) - Former prefectural shrine; associated with the [[Sado Gold Mine]]. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)|ja|大山祇神社 (伊勢市)|12=draft}} (Ise, Mie Prefecture) - A subsidiary shrine within the [[Ise Grand Shrines]] precincts. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nantan)|ja|大山祇神社 (南丹市)|12=draft}} (Nantan, Kyoto Prefecture) - Not dedicated to Ōyamazumi. * {{ill|Bekkyū Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|別宮大山祇神社|1=Betsugu Oyamatsumi Shrine|12=draft}} (Bekkyū, Imabari, Ehime Prefecture) - Former prefectural shrine. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社 (今治市大三島)|Ōyamazumi Shrine=Ōyamazumi Shrine|lt=Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ōmishima)|12=draft}} - Head shrine of all Ōyamazumi shrines nationwide. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishi no Tani)|ja|大山祇神社 (西の谷)|12=draft}} (Niihama, Ehime Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)|ja|大山積神社 (新居浜市)|12=draft}} (Niihama, Ehime Prefecture) - Associated with the [[Besshi Copper Mine]]. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Dōdoigō)|ja|大山祇神社 (新上五島町道土井郷)|12=draft}} (Dōdoigō, Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Imazatogō)|ja|大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)|12=draft}} (Imazatogō, Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Saikai)|ja|大山祇神社 (西海市)|12=draft}} (Saikai, Nagasaki Prefecture) - Associated with the [[Nishisonogi Coalfield]].

'''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社, Ōyamazumi Jinja)
Ōyamazumi Shrine is a [[Shinto shrines]] dedicated to {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|オオヤマツミ|1=Ōyamatsumi|12=draft}}, associated with the [[Mishima and Ōyamazumi faith]]. As of 1973, 897 shrines existed across Japan, from [[Hokkaido]] to [[Kagoshima Prefecture]]. According to a 1995 survey by the Association of Shinto Shrines, there were 293 shrines, while a 2019 study by [[Kanazawa Institute of Technology]] reported 960 shrines. The shrine may also be written as '''Ōyamazumi Shrine''', '''Ōyamatsumi Shrine''', '''Yamazumi Shrine''', or '''Yamatsumi Shrine'''.
The head shrine is the {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社 (今治市大三島)|Ōyamazumi Shrine=Ōyamazumi Shrine|lt=Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ōmishima)|12=draft}} in [[Ōmishima]], [[Imabari]], [[Ehime Prefecture]]. Other related shrine names under the [[Mishima and Ōyamazumi faith]] include {{ill|Mishima Shrine|ja|三島神社|12=draft}}.

'''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社, Ōyamazumi Jinja)
Ōyamazumi Shrine is a [[Shinto shrines]] dedicated to {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|オオヤマツミ|1=Ōyamatsumi|12=draft}}, associated with the [[Mishima and Ōyamazumi faith]]. As of 1973, 897 shrines existed across Japan, from [[Hokkaido]] to [[Kagoshima Prefecture]]. According to a 1995 survey by the Association of Shinto Shrines, there were 293 shrines, while a 2019 study by [[Kanazawa Institute of Technology]] reported 960 shrines. The shrine may also be written as '''Ōyamazumi Shrine''', '''Ōyamatsumi Shrine''', '''Yamazumi Shrine''', or '''Yamatsumi Shrine'''.
The head shrine is the {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社 (今治市大三島)|Ōyamazumi Shrine=Ōyamazumi Shrine|lt=Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ōmishima)|12=draft}} in [[Ōmishima]], [[Imabari]], [[Ehime Prefecture]]. revision.text:
'''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社, Ōyamazumi Jinja)
Ōyamazumi Shrine is a [[Shinto shrines]] dedicated to {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|オオヤマツミ|1=Ōyamatsumi|12=draft|12=draft}}, associated with the [[Mishima and Ōyamazumi faith]]. As of 1973, 897 shrines existed across Japan, from [[Hokkaido]] to [[Kagoshima Prefecture]]. According to a 1995 survey by the Association of Shinto Shrines, there were 293 shrines, while a 2019 study by [[Kanazawa Institute of Technology]] reported 960 shrines. The shrine may also be written as '''Ōyamazumi Shrine''', '''Ōyamatsumi Shrine''', '''Yamazumi Shrine''', or '''Yamatsumi Shrine'''.
The head shrine is the {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社 (今治市大三島)|Ōyamazumi Shrine=Ōyamazumi Shrine|lt=Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ōmishima)|12=draft|12=draft}} in [[Ōmishima]], [[Imabari]], [[Ehime Prefecture]]. Other related shrine names under the [[Mishima and Ōyamazumi faith]] include {{ill|Mishima Shrine|ja|三島神社|12=draft|12=draft}}.

revision.text:
'''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社, Ōyamazumi Jinja)
Ōyamazumi Shrine is a [[Shinto shrines]] dedicated to {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|オオヤマツミ|1=Ōyamatsumi|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, associated with the [[Mishima and Ōyamazumi faith]]. As of 1973, 897 shrines existed across Japan, from [[Hokkaido]] to [[Kagoshima Prefecture]]. According to a 1995 survey by the Association of Shinto Shrines, there were 293 shrines, while a 2019 study by [[Kanazawa Institute of Technology]] reported 960 shrines. The shrine may also be written as '''Ōyamazumi Shrine''', '''Ōyamatsumi Shrine''', '''Yamazumi Shrine''', or '''Yamatsumi Shrine'''.
The head shrine is the {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社 (今治市大三島)|Ōyamazumi Shrine=Ōyamazumi Shrine|lt=Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ōmishima)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in [[Ōmishima]], [[Imabari]], [[Ehime Prefecture]]. '''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社, Ōyamazumi Jinja)
Ōyamazumi Shrine is a [[Shinto shrines]] dedicated to {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|オオヤマツミ|1=Ōyamatsumi|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, associated with the [[Mishima and Ōyamazumi faith]]. As of 1973, 897 shrines existed across Japan, from [[Hokkaido]] to [[Kagoshima Prefecture]]. According to a 1995 survey by the Association of Shinto Shrines, there were 293 shrines, while a 2019 study by [[Kanazawa Institute of Technology]] reported 960 shrines. The shrine may also be written as '''Ōyamazumi Shrine''', '''Ōyamatsumi Shrine''', '''Yamazumi Shrine''', or '''Yamatsumi Shrine'''.
The head shrine is the {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社 (今治市大三島)|Ōyamazumi Shrine=Ōyamazumi Shrine|lt=Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ōmishima)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in [[Ōmishima]], [[Imabari]], [[Ehime Prefecture]]. revision.comment: Text replacement - "Former prefectural" to "Former Prefectural"
revision.text:
'''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社, Ōyamazumi Jinja)
Ōyamazumi Shrine is a [[Shinto shrines]] dedicated to {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|オオヤマツミ|1=Ōyamatsumi|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, associated with the [[Mishima and Ōyamazumi faith]]. As of 1973, 897 shrines existed across Japan, from [[Hokkaido]] to [[Kagoshima Prefecture]]. According to a 1995 survey by the Association of Shinto Shrines, there were 293 shrines, while a 2019 study by [[Kanazawa Institute of Technology]] reported 960 shrines. The shrine may also be written as '''Ōyamazumi Shrine''', '''Ōyamatsumi Shrine''', '''Yamazumi Shrine''', or '''Yamatsumi Shrine'''.
The head shrine is the {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社 (今治市大三島)|Ōyamazumi Shrine=Ōyamazumi Shrine|lt=Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ōmishima)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in [[Ōmishima]], [[Imabari]], [[Ehime Prefecture]]. Other related shrine names under the [[Mishima and Ōyamazumi faith]] include {{ill|Mishima Shrine|ja|三島神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
== Shrines with Articles == * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishiaizu)|ja|大山祇神社 (西会津町)|1=Oyamatsumi Shrine (Nishi Aizu)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Nishiaizu, Fukushima Prefecture) * {{ill|Yamatsumi Shrine (Iitate)|ja|山津見神社 (飯舘村)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Iitate, Fukushima Prefecture) * {{ill|Yamazu Shrine (Hamamatsu)|ja|山住神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Hamamatsu, Shizuoka Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Sado)|ja|大山祇神社 (佐渡市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Sado, Niigata Prefecture) - Former Prefectural shrine; associated with the [[Sado Gold Mine]]. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)|ja|大山祇神社 (伊勢市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Ise, Mie Prefecture) - A subsidiary shrine within the [[Ise Grand Shrines]] precincts. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nantan)|ja|大山祇神社 (南丹市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Nantan, Kyoto Prefecture) - Not dedicated to Ōyamazumi. * {{ill|Bekkyū Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|別宮大山祇神社|1=Betsugu Oyamatsumi Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Bekkyū, Imabari, Ehime Prefecture) - Former Prefectural shrine. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社 (今治市大三島)|Ōyamazumi Shrine=Ōyamazumi Shrine|lt=Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ōmishima)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Head shrine of all Ōyamazumi shrines nationwide. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishi no Tani)|ja|大山祇神社 (西の谷)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Niihama, Ehime Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)|ja|大山積神社 (新居浜市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Niihama, Ehime Prefecture) - Associated with the [[Besshi Copper Mine]]. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Dōdoigō)|ja|大山祇神社 (新上五島町道土井郷)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Dōdoigō, Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Imazatogō)|ja|大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Imazatogō, Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Saikai)|ja|大山祇神社 (西海市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Saikai, Nagasaki Prefecture) - Associated with the [[Nishisonogi Coalfield]].

Other related shrine names under the [[Mishima and Ōyamazumi faith]] include {{ill|Mishima Shrine|ja|三島神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishima Shrine|qq=}}.

revision.text:
'''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社, Ōyamazumi Jinja)
Ōyamazumi Shrine is a [[Shinto shrines]] dedicated to {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|オオヤマツミ|1=Ōyamatsumi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=}}, associated with the [[Mishima and Ōyamazumi faith]]. As of 1973, 897 shrines existed across Japan, from [[Hokkaido]] to [[Kagoshima Prefecture]]. According to a 1995 survey by the Association of Shinto Shrines, there were 293 shrines, while a 2019 study by [[Kanazawa Institute of Technology]] reported 960 shrines. The shrine may also be written as '''Ōyamazumi Shrine''', '''Ōyamatsumi Shrine''', '''Yamazumi Shrine''', or '''Yamatsumi Shrine'''.
The head shrine is the {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社 (今治市大三島)|Ōyamazumi Shrine=Ōyamazumi Shrine|lt=Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ōmishima)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine|qq=}} in [[Ōmishima]], [[Imabari]], [[Ehime Prefecture]]. Other related shrine names under the [[Mishima and Ōyamazumi faith]] include {{ill|Mishima Taisha|lt=Mishima Taisha|lt=Mishima Shrine|ja|三島神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishima Shrine|qq=}}.

revision.text:
'''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社, Ōyamazumi Jinja)
Ōyamazumi Shrine is a [[Shinto shrines]] dedicated to {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|オオヤマツミ|1=Ōyamatsumi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=}}, associated with the [[Mishima and Ōyamazumi faith]]. As of 1973, 897 shrines existed across Japan, from [[Hokkaido]] to [[Kagoshima Prefecture]]. According to a 1995 survey by the Association of Shinto Shrines, there were 293 shrines, while a 2019 study by [[Kanazawa Institute of Technology]] reported 960 shrines. The shrine may also be written as '''Ōyamazumi Shrine''', '''Ōyamatsumi Shrine''', '''Yamazumi Shrine''', or '''Yamatsumi Shrine'''.
The head shrine is the {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社 (今治市大三島)|Ōyamazumi Shrine=Ōyamazumi Shrine|lt=Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ōmishima)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine|qq=}} in [[Ōmishima]], [[Imabari]], [[Ehime Prefecture]]. Other related shrine names under the [[Mishima and Ōyamazumi faith]] include {{ill|Mishima Taisha|lt=Mishima Taisha|lt=Mishima Shrine|ja|三島神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishima Shrine|qq=}}.
== Shrines with Articles == * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishiaizu)|ja|大山祇神社 (西会津町)|1=Oyamatsumi Shrine (Nishi Aizu)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishiaizu)|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyamatsumi Shrine (Nishi Aizu)|qq=}} (Nishiaizu, Fukushima Prefecture) * {{ill|Yamatsumi Shrine (Iitate)|ja|山津見神社 (飯舘村)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatsumi Shrine (Iitate)|qq=}} (Iitate, Fukushima Prefecture) * {{ill|Yamazu Shrine (Hamamatsu)|ja|山住神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamazu Shrine (Hamamatsu)|qq=}} (Hamamatsu, Shizuoka Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Sado)|ja|大山祇神社 (佐渡市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Sado)|qq=}} (Sado, Niigata Prefecture) - Former Prefectural shrine; associated with the [[Sado Gold Mine]]. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)|ja|大山祇神社 (伊勢市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)|qq=}} (Ise, Mie Prefecture) - A subsidiary shrine within the [[Ise Grand Shrines]] precincts. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nantan)|ja|大山祇神社 (南丹市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nantan)|qq=}} (Nantan, Kyoto Prefecture) - Not dedicated to Ōyamazumi. * {{ill|Bekkyū Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|別宮大山祇神社|1=Bekkyū Ōyamazumi Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bekkyū Ōyamazumi Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Betsugu Oyamatsumi Shrine|qq=}} (Bekkyū, Imabari, Ehime Prefecture) - Former Prefectural shrine. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社 (今治市大三島)|Ōyamazumi Shrine=Ōyamazumi Shrine|lt=Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ōmishima)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine|qq=}} - Head shrine of all Ōyamazumi shrines nationwide. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishi no Tani)|ja|大山祇神社 (西の谷)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishi no Tani)|qq=}} (Niihama, Ehime Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)|ja|大山積神社 (新居浜市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)|qq=}} (Niihama, Ehime Prefecture) - Associated with the [[Besshi Copper Mine]]. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Dōdoigō)|ja|大山祇神社 (新上五島町道土井郷)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Dōdoigō)|qq=}} (Dōdoigō, Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Imazatogō)|ja|大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Imazatogō)|qq=}} (Imazatogō, Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Saikai)|ja|大山祇神社 (西海市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Saikai)|qq=}} (Saikai, Nagasaki Prefecture) - Associated with the [[Nishisonogi Coalfield]].

revision.comment: Bot: add category Category:同名の神社
revision.text:
'''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社, Ōyamazumi Jinja)
Ōyamazumi Shrine is a [[Shinto shrines]] dedicated to [[:en:Ōyamatsumi|Ōyamatsumi]], associated with the [[Mishima and Ōyamazumi faith]]. As of 1973, 897 shrines existed across Japan, from [[Hokkaido]] to [[Kagoshima Prefecture]]. According to a 1995 survey by the Association of Shinto Shrines, there were 293 shrines, while a 2019 study by [[Kanazawa Institute of Technology]] reported 960 shrines. The shrine may also be written as '''Ōyamazumi Shrine''', '''Ōyamatsumi Shrine''', '''Yamazumi Shrine''', or '''Yamatsumi Shrine'''.
The head shrine is the {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社 (今治市大三島)|Ōyamazumi Shrine=Ōyamazumi Shrine|lt=Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ōmishima)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine|qq=}} in [[Ōmishima]], [[Imabari]], [[Ehime Prefecture]]. Other related shrine names under the [[Mishima and Ōyamazumi faith]] include {{ill|Mishima Taisha|lt=Mishima Taisha|lt=Mishima Shrine|ja|三島神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishima Shrine|qq=}}.
== Shrines with Articles == * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishiaizu)|ja|大山祇神社 (西会津町)|1=Oyamatsumi Shrine (Nishi Aizu)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishiaizu)|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyamatsumi Shrine (Nishi Aizu)|qq=}} (Nishiaizu, Fukushima Prefecture) * {{ill|Yamatsumi Shrine (Iitate)|ja|山津見神社 (飯舘村)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatsumi Shrine (Iitate)|qq=}} (Iitate, Fukushima Prefecture) * {{ill|Yamazu Shrine (Hamamatsu)|ja|山住神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamazu Shrine (Hamamatsu)|qq=}} (Hamamatsu, Shizuoka Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Sado)|ja|大山祇神社 (佐渡市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Sado)|qq=}} (Sado, Niigata Prefecture) - Former Prefectural shrine; revision.text:
'''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社, Ōyamazumi Jinja)
Ōyamazumi Shrine is a [[Shinto shrines]] dedicated to [[:en:Ōyamatsumi|Ōyamatsumi]], associated with the [[Mishima and Ōyamazumi faith]]. As of 1973, 897 shrines existed across Japan, from [[Hokkaido]] to [[Kagoshima Prefecture]]. According to a 1995 survey by the Association of Shinto Shrines, there were 293 shrines, while a 2019 study by [[Kanazawa Institute of Technology]] reported 960 shrines. The shrine may also be written as '''Ōyamazumi Shrine''', '''Ōyamatsumi Shrine''', '''Yamazumi Shrine''', or '''Yamatsumi Shrine'''.
The head shrine is the {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社 (今治市大三島)|Ōyamazumi Shrine=Ōyamazumi Shrine|lt=Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ōmishima)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine|qq=}} in [[Ōmishima]], [[Imabari]], [[Ehime Prefecture]]. Other related shrine names under the [[Mishima and Ōyamazumi faith]] include {{ill|Mishima Taisha|lt=Mishima Taisha|lt=Mishima Shrine|ja|三島神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishima Shrine|qq=}}.

associated with the [[Sado Gold Mine]]. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)|ja|大山祇神社 (伊勢市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)|qq=}} (Ise, Mie Prefecture) - A subsidiary shrine within the [[Ise Grand Shrines]] precincts. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nantan)|ja|大山祇神社 (南丹市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nantan)|qq=}} (Nantan, Kyoto Prefecture) - Not dedicated to Ōyamazumi. * {{ill|Bekkyū Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|別宮大山祇神社|1=Bekkyū Ōyamazumi Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bekkyū Ōyamazumi Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Betsugu Oyamatsumi Shrine|qq=}} (Bekkyū, Imabari, Ehime Prefecture) - Former Prefectural shrine. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社 (今治市大三島)|Ōyamazumi Shrine=Ōyamazumi Shrine|lt=Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ōmishima)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine|qq=}} - Head shrine of all Ōyamazumi shrines nationwide. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishi no Tani)|ja|大山祇神社 (西の谷)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishi no Tani)|qq=}} (Niihama, Ehime Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)|ja|大山積神社 (新居浜市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)|qq=}} (Niihama, Ehime Prefecture) - Associated with the [[Besshi Copper Mine]]. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Dōdoigō)|ja|大山祇神社 (新上五島町道土井郷)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Dōdoigō)|qq=}} (Dōdoigō, Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Imazatogō)|ja|大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Imazatogō)|qq=}} (Imazatogō, Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Saikai)|ja|大山祇神社 (西海市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Saikai)|qq=}} (Saikai, Nagasaki Prefecture) - Associated with the [[Nishisonogi Coalfield]].

revision.comment: Bot: remove deleted category Completed disambiguations
revision.text:
'''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社, Ōyamazumi Jinja)
Ōyamazumi Shrine is a [[Shinto shrines]] dedicated to [[:en:Ōyamatsumi|Ōyamatsumi]], associated with the [[Mishima and Ōyamazumi faith]]. As of 1973, 897 shrines existed across Japan, from [[Hokkaido]] to [[Kagoshima Prefecture]]. According to a 1995 survey by the Association of Shinto Shrines, there were 293 shrines, while a 2019 study by [[Kanazawa Institute of Technology]] reported 960 shrines. The shrine may also be written as '''Ōyamazumi Shrine''', '''Ōyamatsumi Shrine''', '''Yamazumi Shrine''', or '''Yamatsumi Shrine'''.
The head shrine is the {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社 (今治市大三島)|Ōyamazumi Shrine=Ōyamazumi Shrine|lt=Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ōmishima)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine|qq=}} in [[Ōmishima]], [[Imabari]], [[Ehime Prefecture]]. Other related shrine names under the [[Mishima and Ōyamazumi faith]] include {{ill|Mishima Taisha|lt=Mishima Taisha|lt=Mishima Shrine|ja|三島神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishima Shrine|qq=}}.
== Shrines with Articles == * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishiaizu)|ja|大山祇神社 (西会津町)|1=Oyamatsumi Shrine (Nishi Aizu)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishiaizu)|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyamatsumi Shrine (Nishi Aizu)|qq=}} (Nishiaizu, Fukushima Prefecture) * {{ill|Yamatsumi Shrine (Iitate)|ja|山津見神社 (飯舘村)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatsumi Shrine (Iitate)|qq=}} (Iitate, Fukushima Prefecture) * {{ill|Yamazu Shrine (Hamamatsu)|ja|山住神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamazu Shrine (Hamamatsu)|qq=}} (Hamamatsu, Shizuoka Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Sado)|ja|大山祇神社 (佐渡市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Sado)|qq=}} (Sado, Niigata Prefecture) - Former Prefectural shrine; associated with the [[Sado Gold Mine]]. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)|ja|大山祇神社 (伊勢市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)|qq=}} (Ise, Mie Prefecture) - A subsidiary shrine within the [[Ise Grand Shrines]] precincts. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nantan)|ja|大山祇神社 (南丹市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nantan)|qq=}} (Nantan, Kyoto Prefecture) - Not dedicated to Ōyamazumi. * {{ill|Bekkyū Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|別宮大山祇神社|1=Bekkyū Ōyamazumi Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bekkyū Ōyamazumi Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Betsugu Oyamatsumi Shrine|qq=}} (Bekkyū, Imabari, Ehime Prefecture) - Former Prefectural shrine. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社 (今治市大三島)|Ōyamazumi Shrine=Ōyamazumi Shrine|lt=Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ōmishima)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine|qq=}} - Head shrine of all Ōyamazumi shrines nationwide. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishi no Tani)|ja|大山祇神社 (西の谷)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishi no Tani)|qq=}} (Niihama, Ehime Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)|ja|大山積神社 (新居浜市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)|qq=}} (Niihama, Ehime Prefecture) - Associated with the [[Besshi Copper Mine]]. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Dōdoigō)|ja|大山祇神社 (新上五島町道土井郷)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Dōdoigō)|qq=}} (Dōdoigō, Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Imazatogō)|ja|大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Imazatogō)|qq=}} (Imazatogō, Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Saikai)|ja|大山祇神社 (西海市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Saikai)|qq=}} (Saikai, Nagasaki Prefecture) - Associated with the [[Nishisonogi Coalfield]].

revision.text:
'''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社, Ōyamazumi Jinja)
Ōyamazumi Shrine is a [[Shinto shrines]] dedicated to [[:en:Ōyamatsumi|Ōyamatsumi]], associated with the [[Mishima and Ōyamazumi faith]]. As of 1973, 897 shrines existed across Japan, from [[Hokkaido]] to [[Kagoshima Prefecture]]. According to a 1995 survey by the Association of Shinto Shrines, there were 293 shrines, while a 2019 study by [[Kanazawa Institute of Technology]] reported 960 shrines. The shrine may also be written as '''Ōyamazumi Shrine''', '''Ōyamatsumi Shrine''', '''Yamazumi Shrine''', or '''Yamatsumi Shrine'''.
The head shrine is the {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社 (今治市大三島)|Ōyamazumi Shrine=Ōyamazumi Shrine|lt=Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ōmishima)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[Ōmishima]], [[Imabari]], [[Ehime Prefecture]]. Other related shrine names under the [[Mishima and Ōyamazumi faith]] include {{ill|Mishima Taisha|lt=Mishima Taisha|lt=Mishima Shrine|ja|三島神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishima Shrine|qq=}}.
== Shrines with Articles == * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishiaizu)|ja|大山祇神社 (西会津町)|1=Oyamatsumi Shrine (Nishi Aizu)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishiaizu)|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyamatsumi Shrine (Nishi Aizu)|qq=}} (Nishiaizu, Fukushima Prefecture) * {{ill|Yamatsumi Shrine (Iitate)|ja|山津見神社 (飯舘村)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatsumi Shrine (Iitate)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 山津見神社}} (Iitate, Fukushima Prefecture) * {{ill|Yamazu Shrine (Hamamatsu)|ja|山住神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamazu Shrine (Hamamatsu)|qq=}} (Hamamatsu, Shizuoka Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Sado)|ja|大山祇神社 (佐渡市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Sado)|qq=}} (Sado, Niigata Prefecture) - Former Prefectural shrine; associated with the [[Sado Gold Mine]]. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)|ja|大山祇神社 (伊勢市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)|qq=}} (Ise, Mie Prefecture) - A subsidiary shrine within the [[Ise Grand Shrines]] precincts. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nantan)|ja|大山祇神社 (南丹市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nantan)|qq=}} (Nantan, Kyoto Prefecture) - Not dedicated to Ōyamazumi. * {{ill|Bekkyū Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|別宮大山祇神社|1=Bekkyū Ōyamazumi Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bekkyū Ōyamazumi Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Betsugu Oyamatsumi Shrine|qq=}} (Bekkyū, Imabari, Ehime Prefecture) - Former Prefectural shrine. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社 (今治市大三島)|Ōyamazumi Shrine=Ōyamazumi Shrine|lt=Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ōmishima)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Head shrine of all Ōyamazumi shrines nationwide. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishi no Tani)|ja|大山祇神社 (西の谷)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishi no Tani)|qq=}} (Niihama, Ehime Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)|ja|大山積神社 (新居浜市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)|qq=}} (Niihama, Ehime Prefecture) - Associated with the [[Besshi Copper Mine]]. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Dōdoigō)|ja|大山祇神社 (新上五島町道土井郷)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Dōdoigō)|qq=}} (Dōdoigō, Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Imazatogō)|ja|大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Imazatogō)|qq=}} (Imazatogō, Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Saikai)|ja|大山祇神社 (西海市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Saikai)|qq=}} (Saikai, Nagasaki Prefecture) - Associated with the [[Nishisonogi Coalfield]].

revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text:
'''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社, Ōyamazumi Jinja)
Ōyamazumi Shrine is a [[Shinto shrines]] dedicated to [[:en:Ōyamatsumi|Ōyamatsumi]], associated with the [[Mishima and Ōyamazumi faith]]. As of 1973, 897 shrines existed across Japan, from [[:en:Hokkaidō|Hokkaidō]] to [[Kagoshima Prefecture]]. According to a 1995 survey by the Association of Shinto Shrines, there were 293 shrines, while a 2019 study by [[Kanazawa Institute of Technology]] reported 960 shrines. The shrine may also be written as '''Ōyamazumi Shrine''', '''Ōyamatsumi Shrine''', '''Yamazumi Shrine''', or '''Yamatsumi Shrine'''.
The head shrine is the {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社 (今治市大三島)|Ōyamazumi Shrine=Ōyamazumi Shrine|lt=Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ōmishima)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Ōmishima|Ōmishima]], [[Imabari]], [[:en:Ehime Prefecture|Ehime Prefecture]]. Other related shrine names under the [[Mishima and Ōyamazumi faith]] include {{ill|Mishima Taisha|lt=Mishima Taisha|lt=Mishima Shrine|ja|三島神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishima Shrine|qq=}}.
== Shrines with Articles == * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishiaizu)|ja|大山祇神社 (西会津町)|1=Oyamatsumi Shrine (Nishi Aizu)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishiaizu)|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyamatsumi Shrine (Nishi Aizu)|qq=}} (Nishiaizu, Fukushima Prefecture) * {{ill|Yamatsumi Shrine (Iitate)|ja|山津見神社 (飯舘村)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatsumi Shrine (Iitate)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 山津見神社}} (Iitate, Fukushima Prefecture) * {{ill|Yamazu Shrine (Hamamatsu)|ja|山住神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamazu Shrine (Hamamatsu)|qq=}} (Hamamatsu, Shizuoka Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Sado)|ja|大山祇神社 (佐渡市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Sado)|qq=}} (Sado, Niigata Prefecture) - Former Prefectural shrine; associated with the [[Sado Gold Mine]]. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)|ja|大山祇神社 (伊勢市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)|qq=}} (Ise, Mie Prefecture) - A subsidiary shrine within the [[Ise Grand Shrines]] precincts. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nantan)|ja|大山祇神社 (南丹市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nantan)|qq=}} (Nantan, Kyoto Prefecture) - Not dedicated to Ōyamazumi. * {{ill|Bekkyū Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|別宮大山祇神社|1=Bekkyū Ōyamazumi Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bekkyū Ōyamazumi Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Betsugu Oyamatsumi Shrine|qq=}} (Bekkyū, Imabari, Ehime Prefecture) - Former Prefectural shrine. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社 (今治市大三島)|Ōyamazumi Shrine=Ōyamazumi Shrine|lt=Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ōmishima)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Head shrine of all Ōyamazumi shrines nationwide. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishi no Tani)|ja|大山祇神社 (西の谷)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishi no Tani)|qq=}} (Niihama, Ehime Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)|ja|大山積神社 (新居浜市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)|qq=}} (Niihama, Ehime Prefecture) - Associated with the [[Besshi Copper Mine]]. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Dōdoigō)|ja|大山祇神社 (新上五島町道土井郷)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Dōdoigō)|qq=}} (Dōdoigō, Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Imazatogō)|ja|大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Imazatogō)|qq=}} (Imazatogō, Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Saikai)|ja|大山祇神社 (西海市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Saikai)|qq=}} (Saikai, Nagasaki Prefecture) - Associated with the [[Nishisonogi Coalfield]].

revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text:
'''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社, Ōyamazumi Jinja)
Ōyamazumi Shrine is a [[Shinto shrines]] dedicated to [[:en:Ōyamatsumi|Ōyamatsumi]], associated with the [[Mishima and Ōyamazumi faith]]. As of 1973, 897 shrines existed across Japan, from [[:en:Hokkaidō|Hokkaidō]] to [[:en:Kagoshima Prefecture|Kagoshima Prefecture]]. According to a 1995 survey by the Association of Shinto Shrines, there were 293 shrines, while a 2019 study by [[Kanazawa Institute of Technology]] reported 960 shrines. The shrine may also be written as '''Ōyamazumi Shrine''', '''Ōyamatsumi Shrine''', '''Yamazumi Shrine''', or '''Yamatsumi Shrine'''.
The head shrine is the {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社 (今治市大三島)|Ōyamazumi Shrine=Ōyamazumi Shrine|lt=Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ōmishima)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Ōmishima|Ōmishima]], [[:en:Imabari|Imabari]], [[:en:Ehime Prefecture|Ehime Prefecture]]. Other related shrine names under the [[Mishima and Ōyamazumi faith]] include {{ill|Mishima Taisha|lt=Mishima Taisha|lt=Mishima Shrine|ja|三島神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishima Shrine|qq=}}.
== Shrines with Articles == * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishiaizu)|ja|大山祇神社 (西会津町)|1=Oyamatsumi Shrine (Nishi Aizu)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishiaizu)|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyamatsumi Shrine (Nishi Aizu)|qq=}} (Nishiaizu, Fukushima Prefecture) * {{ill|Yamatsumi Shrine (Iitate)|ja|山津見神社 (飯舘村)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatsumi Shrine (Iitate)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 山津見神社}} (Iitate, Fukushima Prefecture) * {{ill|Yamazu Shrine (Hamamatsu)|ja|山住神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamazu Shrine (Hamamatsu)|qq=}} (Hamamatsu, Shizuoka Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Sado)|ja|大山祇神社 (佐渡市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Sado)|qq=}} (Sado, Niigata Prefecture) - Former Prefectural shrine; associated with the [[Sado Gold Mine]]. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)|ja|大山祇神社 (伊勢市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)|qq=}} (Ise, Mie Prefecture) - A subsidiary shrine within the [[Ise Grand Shrines]] precincts. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nantan)|ja|大山祇神社 (南丹市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nantan)|qq=}} (Nantan, Kyoto Prefecture) - Not dedicated to Ōyamazumi. * {{ill|Bekkyū Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|別宮大山祇神社|1=Bekkyū Ōyamazumi Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bekkyū Ōyamazumi Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Betsugu Oyamatsumi Shrine|qq=}} (Bekkyū, Imabari, Ehime Prefecture) - Former Prefectural shrine. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社 (今治市大三島)|Ōyamazumi Shrine=Ōyamazumi Shrine|lt=Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ōmishima)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Head shrine of all Ōyamazumi shrines nationwide. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishi no Tani)|ja|大山祇神社 (西の谷)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishi no Tani)|qq=}} (Niihama, Ehime Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)|ja|大山積神社 (新居浜市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)|qq=}} (Niihama, Ehime Prefecture) - Associated with the [[Besshi Copper Mine]]. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Dōdoigō)|ja|大山祇神社 (新上五島町道土井郷)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Dōdoigō)|qq=}} (Dōdoigō, Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Imazatogō)|ja|大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Imazatogō)|qq=}} (Imazatogō, Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Saikai)|ja|大山祇神社 (西海市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Saikai)|qq=}} (Saikai, Nagasaki Prefecture) - Associated with the [[Nishisonogi Coalfield]].

revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q16278443]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:43, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社, Ōyamazumi Jinja)
Ōyamazumi Shrine is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] dedicated to [[:en:Ōyamatsumi|Ōyamatsumi]], associated with the [[Mishima and Ōyamazumi faith]]. As of 1973, 897 shrines existed across Japan, from [[:en:Hokkaidō|Hokkaidō]] to [[:en:Kagoshima Prefecture|Kagoshima Prefecture]]. According to a 1995 survey by the Association of Shinto Shrines, there were 293 shrines, while a 2019 study by [[:en:Kanazawa Institute of Technology|Kanazawa Institute of Technology]] reported 960 shrines. The shrine may also be written as '''Ōyamazumi Shrine''', '''Ōyamatsumi Shrine''', '''Yamazumi Shrine''', or '''Yamatsumi Shrine'''.
The head shrine is the {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社 (今治市大三島)|Ōyamazumi Shrine=Ōyamazumi Shrine|lt=Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ōmishima)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Ōmishima|Ōmishima]], [[:en:Imabari|Imabari]], [[:en:Ehime Prefecture|Ehime Prefecture]]. Other related shrine names under the [[Mishima and Ōyamazumi faith]] include {{ill|Mishima Taisha|lt=Mishima Taisha|lt=Mishima Shrine|ja|三島神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishima Shrine|qq=}}.

associated with the [[Sado Gold Mine]]. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)|ja|大山祇神社 (伊勢市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)|qq=}} (Ise, Mie Prefecture) - A subsidiary shrine within the [[Ise Grand Shrines]] precincts. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nantan)|ja|大山祇神社 (南丹市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nantan)|qq=}} (Nantan, Kyoto Prefecture) - Not dedicated to Ōyamazumi. * {{ill|Bekkyū Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|別宮大山祇神社|1=Bekkyū Ōyamazumi Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bekkyū Ōyamazumi Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Betsugu Oyamatsumi Shrine|qq=}} (Bekkyū, Imabari, Ehime Prefecture) - Former Prefectural shrine. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社 (今治市大三島)|Ōyamazumi Shrine=Ōyamazumi Shrine|lt=Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ōmishima)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Head shrine of all Ōyamazumi shrines nationwide. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishi no Tani)|ja|大山祇神社 (西の谷)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishi no Tani)|qq=}} (Niihama, Ehime Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)|ja|大山積神社 (新居浜市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)|qq=}} (Niihama, Ehime Prefecture) - Associated with the [[Besshi Copper Mine]]. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Dōdoigō)|ja|大山祇神社 (新上五島町道土井郷)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Dōdoigō)|qq=}} (Dōdoigō, Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Imazatogō)|ja|大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Imazatogō)|qq=}} (Imazatogō, Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Saikai)|ja|大山祇神社 (西海市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Saikai)|qq=}} (Saikai, Nagasaki Prefecture) - Associated with the [[Nishisonogi Coalfield]].

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q16278443]] Wikidata name: [[Category:Add wikidata]] [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:43, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社, Ōyamazumi Jinja)
Ōyamazumi Shrine is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] dedicated to [[:en:Ōyamatsumi|Ōyamatsumi]], associated with the [[Mishima–Ōyamazumi cult]]. As of 1973, 897 shrines existed across Japan, from [[:en:Hokkaidō|Hokkaidō]] to [[:en:Kagoshima Prefecture|Kagoshima Prefecture]]. According to a 1995 survey by the Association of Shinto Shrines, there were 293 shrines, while a 2019 study by [[:en:Kanazawa Institute of Technology|Kanazawa Institute of Technology]] reported 960 shrines. The shrine may also be written as '''Ōyamazumi Shrine''', '''Ōyamatsumi Shrine''', '''Yamazumi Shrine''', or '''Yamatsumi Shrine'''.
The head shrine is the {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社 (今治市大三島)|Ōyamazumi Shrine=Ōyamazumi Shrine|lt=Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ōmishima)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Ōmishima|Ōmishima]], [[:en:Imabari|Imabari]], [[:en:Ehime Prefecture|Ehime Prefecture]]. Other related shrine names under the [[Mishima–Ōyamazumi cult]] include {{ill|Mishima Taisha|lt=Mishima Taisha|lt=Mishima Shrine|ja|三島神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishima Shrine|qq=}}.

associated with the [[Sado Gold Mine]]. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)|ja|大山祇神社 (伊勢市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)|qq=}} (Ise, Mie Prefecture) - A subsidiary shrine within the [[Ise Grand Shrines]] precincts. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nantan)|ja|大山祇神社 (南丹市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nantan)|qq=}} (Nantan, Kyoto Prefecture) - Not dedicated to Ōyamazumi. * {{ill|Bekkyū Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|別宮大山祇神社|1=Bekkyū Ōyamazumi Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bekkyū Ōyamazumi Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Betsugu Oyamatsumi Shrine|qq=}} (Bekkyū, Imabari, Ehime Prefecture) - Former Prefectural shrine. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社 (今治市大三島)|Ōyamazumi Shrine=Ōyamazumi Shrine|lt=Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ōmishima)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Head shrine of all Ōyamazumi shrines nationwide. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishi no Tani)|ja|大山祇神社 (西の谷)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishi no Tani)|qq=}} (Niihama, Ehime Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)|ja|大山積神社 (新居浜市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)|qq=}} (Niihama, Ehime Prefecture) - Associated with the [[Besshi Copper Mine]]. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Dōdoigō)|ja|大山祇神社 (新上五島町道土井郷)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Dōdoigō)|qq=}} (Dōdoigō, Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Imazatogō)|ja|大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Imazatogō)|qq=}} (Imazatogō, Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Saikai)|ja|大山祇神社 (西海市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Saikai)|qq=}} (Saikai, Nagasaki Prefecture) - Associated with the [[Nishisonogi Coalfield]].

The head shrine is the {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社 (今治市大三島)|Ōyamazumi Shrine=Ōyamazumi Shrine|lt=Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ōmishima)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Ōmishima|Ōmishima]], [[:en:Imabari|Imabari]], [[:en:Ehime Prefecture|Ehime Prefecture]]. Ōyamazumi Shrine is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] dedicated to [[:en:Ōyamatsumi|Ōyamatsumi]], associated with the [[Mishima–Ōyamazumi cult]]. As of 1973, 897 shrines existed across Japan, from [[:en:Hokkaidō|Hokkaidō]] to [[:en:Kagoshima Prefecture|Kagoshima Prefecture]]. According to a 1995 survey by the Association of Shinto Shrines, there were 293 shrines, while a 2019 study by [[:en:Kanazawa Institute of Technology|Kanazawa Institute of Technology]] reported 960 shrines. revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:Qq]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q16278443]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:43, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社, Ōyamazumi Jinja)
Ōyamazumi Shrine is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] dedicated to [[:en:Ōyamatsumi|Ōyamatsumi]], associated with the [[Mishima–Ōyamazumi cult]]. As of 1973, 897 shrines existed across Japan, from [[:en:Hokkaidō|Hokkaidō]] to [[:en:Kagoshima Prefecture|Kagoshima Prefecture]]. According to a 1995 survey by the Association of Shinto Shrines, there were 293 shrines, while a 2019 study by [[:en:Kanazawa Institute of Technology|Kanazawa Institute of Technology]] reported 960 shrines. The shrine may also be written as '''Ōyamazumi Shrine''', '''Ōyamatsumi Shrine''', '''Yamazumi Shrine''', or '''Yamatsumi Shrine'''.
The head shrine is the {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社 (今治市大三島)|Ōyamazumi Shrine=Ōyamazumi Shrine|lt=Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ōmishima)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Ōmishima|Ōmishima]], [[:en:Imabari|Imabari]], [[:en:Ehime Prefecture|Ehime Prefecture]]. Other related shrine names under the [[Mishima–Ōyamazumi cult]] include {{ill|Mishima Taisha|lt=Mishima Taisha|lt=Mishima Shrine|ja|三島神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishima Shrine|qq=}}.
