associated with the [[Sado Gold Mine]]. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)|ja|大山祇神社 (伊勢市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)|qq=}} (Ise, Mie Prefecture) - A subsidiary shrine within the [[Ise Grand Shrines]] precincts. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nantan)|ja|大山祇神社 (南丹市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nantan)|qq=}} (Nantan, Kyoto Prefecture) - Not dedicated to Ōyamazumi. * {{ill|Bekkyū Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|別宮大山祇神社|1=Bekkyū Ōyamazumi Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bekkyū Ōyamazumi Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Betsugu Oyamatsumi Shrine|qq=}} (Bekkyū, Imabari, Ehime Prefecture) - Former Prefectural shrine. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社 (今治市大三島)|Ōyamazumi Shrine=Ōyamazumi Shrine|lt=Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ōmishima)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Head shrine of all Ōyamazumi shrines nationwide. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishi no Tani)|ja|大山祇神社 (西の谷)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishi no Tani)|qq=}} (Niihama, Ehime Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)|ja|大山積神社 (新居浜市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)|qq=}} (Niihama, Ehime Prefecture) - Associated with the [[Besshi Copper Mine]]. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Dōdoigō)|ja|大山祇神社 (新上五島町道土井郷)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Dōdoigō)|qq=}} (Dōdoigō, Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Imazatogō)|ja|大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Imazatogō)|qq=}} (Imazatogō, Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Saikai)|ja|大山祇神社 (西海市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Saikai)|qq=}} (Saikai, Nagasaki Prefecture) - Associated with the [[Nishisonogi Coalfield]].

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q16278443]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:43, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社, Ōyamazumi Jinja)
Ōyamazumi Shrine is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] dedicated to [[:en:Ōyamatsumi|Ōyamatsumi]], associated with the [[Mishima–Ōyamazumi cult]]. As of 1973, 897 shrines existed across Japan, from [[:en:Hokkaidō|Hokkaidō]] to [[:en:Kagoshima Prefecture|Kagoshima Prefecture]]. According to a 1995 survey by the Association of Shinto Shrines, there were 293 shrines, while a 2019 study by [[:en:Kanazawa Institute of Technology|Kanazawa Institute of Technology]] reported 960 shrines. The shrine may also be written as '''Ōyamazumi Shrine''', '''Ōyamatsumi Shrine''', '''Yamazumi Shrine''', or '''Yamatsumi Shrine'''.
The head shrine is the {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社 (今治市大三島)|Ōyamazumi Shrine=Ōyamazumi Shrine|lt=Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ōmishima)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Ōmishima|Ōmishima]], [[:en:Imabari|Imabari]], [[:en:Ehime Prefecture|Ehime Prefecture]]. Other related shrine names under the [[Mishima–Ōyamazumi cult]] include {{ill|Mishima Taisha|lt=Mishima Taisha|lt=Mishima Shrine|ja|三島神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishima Shrine|qq=}}.

associated with the [[Sado Gold Mine]]. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)|ja|大山祇神社 (伊勢市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)|qq=}} (Ise, Mie Prefecture) - A subsidiary shrine within the [[Ise Grand Shrines]] precincts. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nantan)|ja|大山祇神社 (南丹市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nantan)|qq=}} (Nantan, Kyoto Prefecture) - Not dedicated to Ōyamazumi. * {{ill|Bekkyū Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|別宮大山祇神社|1=Bekkyū Ōyamazumi Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bekkyū Ōyamazumi Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Betsugu Oyamatsumi Shrine|qq=}} (Bekkyū, Imabari, Ehime Prefecture) - Former Prefectural shrine. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社 (今治市大三島)|Ōyamazumi Shrine=Ōyamazumi Shrine|lt=Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ōmishima)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Head shrine of all Ōyamazumi shrines nationwide. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishi no Tani)|ja|大山祇神社 (西の谷)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishi no Tani)|qq=}} (Niihama, Ehime Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)|ja|大山積神社 (新居浜市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)|qq=}} (Niihama, Ehime Prefecture) - Associated with the [[Besshi Copper Mine]]. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Dōdoigō)|ja|大山祇神社 (新上五島町道土井郷)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Dōdoigō)|qq=}} (Dōdoigō, Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Imazatogō)|ja|大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Imazatogō)|qq=}} (Imazatogō, Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Saikai)|ja|大山祇神社 (西海市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Saikai)|qq=}} (Saikai, Nagasaki Prefecture) - Associated with the [[Nishisonogi Coalfield]].

revision.comment: Text replacement - "user:immanuelle" to "User:Immanuelle"
revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q16278443]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:43, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社, Ōyamazumi Jinja)
Ōyamazumi Shrine is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] dedicated to [[:en:Ōyamatsumi|Ōyamatsumi]], associated with the [[Mishima–Ōyamazumi cult]]. As of 1973, 897 shrines existed across Japan, from [[:en:Hokkaidō|Hokkaidō]] to [[:en:Kagoshima Prefecture|Kagoshima Prefecture]]. According to a 1995 survey by the Association of Shinto Shrines, there were 293 shrines, while a 2019 study by [[:en:Kanazawa Institute of Technology|Kanazawa Institute of Technology]] reported 960 shrines. The shrine may also be written as '''Ōyamazumi Shrine''', '''Ōyamatsumi Shrine''', '''Yamazumi Shrine''', or '''Yamatsumi Shrine'''.
The head shrine is the {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社 (今治市大三島)|Ōyamazumi Shrine=Ōyamazumi Shrine|lt=Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ōmishima)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Ōmishima|Ōmishima]], [[:en:Imabari|Imabari]], [[:en:Ehime Prefecture|Ehime Prefecture]]. Other related shrine names under the [[Mishima–Ōyamazumi cult]] include {{ill|Mishima Taisha|lt=Mishima Taisha|lt=Mishima Shrine|ja|三島神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishima Shrine|qq=}}.
== Shrines with Articles == * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishiaizu)|ja|大山祇神社 (西会津町)|1=Oyamatsumi Shrine (Nishi Aizu)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishiaizu)|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyamatsumi Shrine (Nishi Aizu)|qq=}} (Nishiaizu, Fukushima Prefecture) * {{ill|Yamatsumi Shrine (Iitate)|ja|山津見神社 (飯舘村)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatsumi Shrine (Iitate)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 山津見神社|1=Yamatsumi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11469987}} (Iitate, Fukushima Prefecture) * {{ill|Yamazu Shrine (Hamamatsu)|ja|山住神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamazu Shrine (Hamamatsu)|qq=|1=Yamazumi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11466290}} (Hamamatsu, Shizuoka Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Sado)|ja|大山祇神社 (佐渡市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Sado)|qq=|1=Ōyamazumi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q124496789}} (Sado, Niigata Prefecture) - Former Prefectural shrine; associated with the [[Sado Gold Mine]]. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)|ja|大山祇神社 (伊勢市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)|qq=}} (Ise, Mie Prefecture) - A subsidiary shrine within the [[Ise Grand Shrines]] precincts. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nantan)|ja|大山祇神社 (南丹市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nantan)|qq=}} (Nantan, Kyoto Prefecture) - Not dedicated to Ōyamazumi. * {{ill|Bekkyū Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|別宮大山祇神社|1=Bekkyū Ōyamazumi Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bekkyū Ōyamazumi Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Betsugu Oyamatsumi Shrine|qq=}} (Bekkyū, Imabari, Ehime Prefecture) - Former Prefectural shrine. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社 (今治市大三島)|Ōyamazumi Shrine=Ōyamazumi Shrine|lt=Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ōmishima)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Head shrine of all Ōyamazumi shrines nationwide. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishi no Tani)|ja|大山祇神社 (西の谷)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishi no Tani)|qq=}} (Niihama, Ehime Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)|ja|大山積神社 (新居浜市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)|qq=}} (Niihama, Ehime Prefecture) - Associated with the [[Besshi Copper Mine]]. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Dōdoigō)|ja|大山祇神社 (新上五島町道土井郷)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Dōdoigō)|qq=}} (Dōdoigō, Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Imazatogō)|ja|大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Imazatogō)|qq=}} (Imazatogō, Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Saikai)|ja|大山祇神社 (西海市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Saikai)|qq=}} (Saikai, Nagasaki Prefecture) - Associated with the [[Nishisonogi Coalfield]].

revision.comment: Adding [[Category:pages with former wikipedia userpage links]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q16278443]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:43, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社, Ōyamazumi Jinja)
Ōyamazumi Shrine is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] dedicated to [[:en:Ōyamatsumi|Ōyamatsumi]], associated with the [[Mishima–Ōyamazumi cult]]. As of 1973, 897 shrines existed across Japan, from [[:en:Hokkaidō|Hokkaidō]] to [[:en:Kagoshima Prefecture|Kagoshima Prefecture]]. According to a 1995 survey by the Association of Shinto Shrines, there were 293 shrines, while a 2019 study by [[:en:Kanazawa Institute of Technology|Kanazawa Institute of Technology]] reported 960 shrines. The shrine may also be written as '''Ōyamazumi Shrine''', '''Ōyamatsumi Shrine''', '''Yamazumi Shrine''', or '''Yamatsumi Shrine'''.
The head shrine is the {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社 (今治市大三島)|Ōyamazumi Shrine=Ōyamazumi Shrine|lt=Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ōmishima)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Ōmishima|Ōmishima]], [[:en:Imabari|Imabari]], [[:en:Ehime Prefecture|Ehime Prefecture]]. Other related shrine names under the [[Mishima–Ōyamazumi cult]] include {{ill|Mishima Taisha|lt=Mishima Taisha|lt=Mishima Shrine|ja|三島神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishima Shrine|qq=}}.
== Shrines with Articles == * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishiaizu)|ja|大山祇神社 (西会津町)|1=Oyamatsumi Shrine (Nishi Aizu)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishiaizu)|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyamatsumi Shrine (Nishi Aizu)|qq=}} (Nishiaizu, Fukushima Prefecture) * {{ill|Yamatsumi Shrine (Iitate)|ja|山津見神社 (飯舘村)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatsumi Shrine (Iitate)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 山津見神社|1=Yamatsumi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11469987}} (Iitate, Fukushima Prefecture) * {{ill|Yamazu Shrine (Hamamatsu)|ja|山住神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamazu Shrine (Hamamatsu)|qq=|1=Yamazumi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11466290}} (Hamamatsu, Shizuoka Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Sado)|ja|大山祇神社 (佐渡市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Sado)|qq=|1=Ōyamazumi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q124496789}} (Sado, Niigata Prefecture) - Former Prefectural shrine; associated with the [[Sado Gold Mine]]. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)|ja|大山祇神社 (伊勢市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)|qq=}} (Ise, Mie Prefecture) - A subsidiary shrine within the [[Ise Grand Shrines]] precincts. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nantan)|ja|大山祇神社 (南丹市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nantan)|qq=}} (Nantan, Kyoto Prefecture) - Not dedicated to Ōyamazumi. * {{ill|Bekkyū Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|別宮大山祇神社|1=Bekkyū Ōyamazumi Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bekkyū Ōyamazumi Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Betsugu Oyamatsumi Shrine|qq=}} (Bekkyū, Imabari, Ehime Prefecture) - Former Prefectural shrine. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社 (今治市大三島)|Ōyamazumi Shrine=Ōyamazumi Shrine|lt=Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ōmishima)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Head shrine of all Ōyamazumi shrines nationwide. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishi no Tani)|ja|大山祇神社 (西の谷)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishi no Tani)|qq=}} (Niihama, Ehime Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)|ja|大山積神社 (新居浜市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)|qq=}} (Niihama, Ehime Prefecture) - Associated with the [[Besshi Copper Mine]]. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Dōdoigō)|ja|大山祇神社 (新上五島町道土井郷)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Dōdoigō)|qq=}} (Dōdoigō, Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Imazatogō)|ja|大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Imazatogō)|qq=}} (Imazatogō, Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Saikai)|ja|大山祇神社 (西海市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Saikai)|qq=}} (Saikai, Nagasaki Prefecture) - Associated with the [[Nishisonogi Coalfield]].

revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q16278443]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:43, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社, Ōyamazumi Jinja)
Ōyamazumi Shrine is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] dedicated to [[:en:Ōyamatsumi|Ōyamatsumi]], associated with the [[Mishima–Ōyamazumi cult]]. As of 1973, 897 shrines existed across Japan, from [[:en:Hokkaidō|Hokkaidō]] to [[:en:Kagoshima Prefecture|Kagoshima Prefecture]]. According to a 1995 survey by the Association of Shinto Shrines, there were 293 shrines, while a 2019 study by [[:en:Kanazawa Institute of Technology|Kanazawa Institute of Technology]] reported 960 shrines. The shrine may also be written as '''Ōyamazumi Shrine''', '''Ōyamatsumi Shrine''', '''Yamazumi Shrine''', or '''Yamatsumi Shrine'''.
The head shrine is the {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社 (今治市大三島)|Ōyamazumi Shrine=Ōyamazumi Shrine|lt=Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ōmishima)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Ōmishima|Ōmishima]], [[:en:Imabari|Imabari]], [[:en:Ehime Prefecture|Ehime Prefecture]]. Other related shrine names under the [[Mishima–Ōyamazumi cult]] include {{ill|Mishima Taisha|lt=Mishima Taisha|lt=Mishima Shrine|ja|三島神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishima Shrine|qq=}}.
== Shrines with Articles == * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishiaizu)|ja|大山祇神社 (西会津町)|1=Oyamatsumi Shrine (Nishi Aizu)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishiaizu)|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyamatsumi Shrine (Nishi Aizu)|qq=}} (Nishiaizu, Fukushima Prefecture) * {{ill|Yamatsumi Shrine (Iitate)|ja|山津見神社 (飯舘村)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatsumi Shrine (Iitate)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 山津見神社|1=Yamatsumi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11469987}} (Iitate, Fukushima Prefecture) * {{ill|Yamazu Shrine (Hamamatsu)|ja|山住神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamazu Shrine (Hamamatsu)|qq=|1=Yamazumi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11466290}} (Hamamatsu, Shizuoka Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Sado)|ja|大山祇神社 (佐渡市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Sado)|qq=|1=Ōyamazumi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q124496789}} (Sado, Niigata Prefecture) - Former Prefectural shrine; associated with the [[Sado Gold Mine]]. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)|ja|大山祇神社 (伊勢市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)|qq=}} (Ise, Mie Prefecture) - A subsidiary shrine within the [[Ise Grand Shrines]] precincts. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nantan)|ja|大山祇神社 (南丹市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nantan)|qq=}} (Nantan, Kyoto Prefecture) - Not dedicated to Ōyamazumi. * {{ill|Bekkyū Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|別宮大山祇神社|1=Bekkyū Ōyamazumi Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bekkyū Ōyamazumi Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Betsugu Oyamatsumi Shrine|qq=}} (Bekkyū, Imabari, Ehime Prefecture) - Former Prefectural shrine. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社 (今治市大三島)|Ōyamazumi Shrine=Ōyamazumi Shrine|lt=Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ōmishima)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Head shrine of all Ōyamazumi shrines nationwide. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishi no Tani)|ja|大山祇神社 (西の谷)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishi no Tani)|qq=}} (Niihama, Ehime Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)|ja|大山積神社 (新居浜市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)|qq=}} (Niihama, Ehime Prefecture) - Associated with the [[Besshi Copper Mine]]. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Dōdoigō)|ja|大山祇神社 (新上五島町道土井郷)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Dōdoigō)|qq=}} (Dōdoigō, Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Imazatogō)|ja|大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Imazatogō)|qq=}} (Imazatogō, Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Saikai)|ja|大山祇神社 (西海市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Saikai)|qq=}} (Saikai, Nagasaki Prefecture) - Associated with the [[Nishisonogi Coalfield]].

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q16278443]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:43, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社, Ōyamazumi Jinja)
Ōyamazumi Shrine is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] dedicated to [[:en:Ōyamatsumi|Ōyamatsumi]], associated with the [[Mishima–Ōyamazumi cult]]. As of 1973, 897 shrines existed across Japan, from [[:en:Hokkaidō|Hokkaidō]] to [[:en:Kagoshima Prefecture|Kagoshima Prefecture]]. According to a 1995 survey by the Association of Shinto Shrines, there were 293 shrines, while a 2019 study by [[:en:Kanazawa Institute of Technology|Kanazawa Institute of Technology]] reported 960 shrines. The shrine may also be written as '''Ōyamazumi Shrine''', '''Ōyamatsumi Shrine''', '''Yamazumi Shrine''', or '''Yamatsumi Shrine'''.
The head shrine is the {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社 (今治市大三島)|Ōyamazumi Shrine=Ōyamazumi Shrine|lt=Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ōmishima)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Ōmishima|Ōmishima]], [[:en:Imabari|Imabari]], [[:en:Ehime Prefecture|Ehime Prefecture]]. Other related shrine names under the [[Mishima–Ōyamazumi cult]] include {{ill|Mishima Taisha|lt=Mishima Taisha|lt=Mishima Shrine|ja|三島神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishima Shrine|qq=}}.

associated with the [[Sado Gold Mine]]. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)|ja|大山祇神社 (伊勢市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)|qq=}} (Ise, Mie Prefecture) - A subsidiary shrine within the [[Ise Grand Shrines]] precincts. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nantan)|ja|大山祇神社 (南丹市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nantan)|qq=}} (Nantan, Kyoto Prefecture) - Not dedicated to Ōyamazumi. * {{ill|Bekkyū Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|別宮大山祇神社|1=Bekkyū Ōyamazumi Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bekkyū Ōyamazumi Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Betsugu Oyamatsumi Shrine|qq=}} (Bekkyū, Imabari, Ehime Prefecture) - Former Prefectural shrine. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社 (今治市大三島)|Ōyamazumi Shrine=Ōyamazumi Shrine|lt=Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ōmishima)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Head shrine of all Ōyamazumi shrines nationwide. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishi no Tani)|ja|大山祇神社 (西の谷)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishi no Tani)|qq=}} (Niihama, Ehime Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)|ja|大山積神社 (新居浜市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)|qq=}} (Niihama, Ehime Prefecture) - Associated with the [[Besshi Copper Mine]]. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Dōdoigō)|ja|大山祇神社 (新上五島町道土井郷)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Dōdoigō)|qq=}} (Dōdoigō, Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Imazatogō)|ja|大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Imazatogō)|qq=}} (Imazatogō, Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Saikai)|ja|大山祇神社 (西海市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Saikai)|qq=}} (Saikai, Nagasaki Prefecture) - Associated with the [[Nishisonogi Coalfield]].

revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q16278443]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:43, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社, Ōyamazumi Jinja)
Ōyamazumi Shrine is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] dedicated to [[:en:Ōyamatsumi|Ōyamatsumi]], associated with the [[Mishima–Ōyamazumi cult]]. As of 1973, 897 shrines existed across Japan, from [[:en:Hokkaidō|Hokkaidō]] to [[:en:Kagoshima Prefecture|Kagoshima Prefecture]]. According to a 1995 survey by the Association of Shinto Shrines, there were 293 shrines, while a 2019 study by [[:en:Kanazawa Institute of Technology|Kanazawa Institute of Technology]] reported 960 shrines. The shrine may also be written as '''Ōyamazumi Shrine''', '''Ōyamatsumi Shrine''', '''Yamazumi Shrine''', or '''Yamatsumi Shrine'''.
The head shrine is the {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社 (今治市大三島)|Ōyamazumi Shrine=Ōyamazumi Shrine|lt=Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ōmishima)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Ōmishima|Ōmishima]], [[:en:Imabari|Imabari]], [[:en:Ehime Prefecture|Ehime Prefecture]]. Other related shrine names under the [[Mishima–Ōyamazumi cult]] include {{ill|Mishima Taisha|lt=Mishima Taisha|lt=Mishima Shrine|ja|三島神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishima Shrine|qq=}}.

associated with the [[Sado Gold Mine]]. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)|ja|大山祇神社 (伊勢市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)|qq=}} (Ise, Mie Prefecture) - A subsidiary shrine within the [[Ise Grand Shrines]] precincts. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nantan)|ja|大山祇神社 (南丹市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nantan)|qq=}} (Nantan, Kyoto Prefecture) - Not dedicated to Ōyamazumi. * {{ill|Bekkyū Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|別宮大山祇神社|1=Bekkyū Ōyamazumi Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bekkyū Ōyamazumi Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Betsugu Oyamatsumi Shrine|qq=}} (Bekkyū, Imabari, Ehime Prefecture) - Former Prefectural shrine. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社 (今治市大三島)|Ōyamazumi Shrine=Ōyamazumi Shrine|lt=Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ōmishima)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Head shrine of all Ōyamazumi shrines nationwide. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishi no Tani)|ja|大山祇神社 (西の谷)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishi no Tani)|qq=}} (Niihama, Ehime Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)|ja|大山積神社 (新居浜市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)|qq=}} (Niihama, Ehime Prefecture) - Associated with the [[Besshi Copper Mine]]. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Michidoi, Shinkamigoto)|ja|大山祇神社 (新上五島町道土井郷)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Michidoi, Shinkamigoto)|qq=}} (Dōdoigō, Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Imazatogō)|ja|大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Imazatogō)|qq=}} (Imazatogō, Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Saikai)|ja|大山祇神社 (西海市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Saikai)|qq=}} (Saikai, Nagasaki Prefecture) - Associated with the [[Nishisonogi Coalfield]].

revision.comment: Bot: add interwikis from Wikidata
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大山祇神社 (曖昧さ回避)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q16278443]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:43, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社, Ōyamazumi Jinja)
Ōyamazumi Shrine is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] dedicated to [[:en:Ōyamatsumi|Ōyamatsumi]], associated with the [[Mishima–Ōyamazumi cult]]. As of 1973, 897 shrines existed across Japan, from [[:en:Hokkaidō|Hokkaidō]] to [[:en:Kagoshima Prefecture|Kagoshima Prefecture]]. According to a 1995 survey by the Association of Shinto Shrines, there were 293 shrines, while a 2019 study by [[:en:Kanazawa Institute of Technology|Kanazawa Institute of Technology]] reported 960 shrines. The shrine may also be written as '''Ōyamazumi Shrine''', '''Ōyamatsumi Shrine''', '''Yamazumi Shrine''', or '''Yamatsumi Shrine'''.
The head shrine is the {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社 (今治市大三島)|Ōyamazumi Shrine=Ōyamazumi Shrine|lt=Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ōmishima)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Ōmishima|Ōmishima]], [[:en:Imabari|Imabari]], [[:en:Ehime Prefecture|Ehime Prefecture]]. Other related shrine names under the [[Mishima–Ōyamazumi cult]] include {{ill|Mishima Taisha|lt=Mishima Taisha|lt=Mishima Shrine|ja|三島神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishima Shrine|qq=}}.
== Shrines with Articles == * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishiaizu)|ja|大山祇神社 (西会津町)|1=Oyamatsumi Shrine (Nishi Aizu)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishiaizu)|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyamatsumi Shrine (Nishi Aizu)|qq=}} (Nishiaizu, Fukushima Prefecture) * {{ill|Yamatsumi Shrine (Iitate)|ja|山津見神社 (飯舘村)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatsumi Shrine (Iitate)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 山津見神社|1=Yamatsumi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11469987}} (Iitate, Fukushima Prefecture) * {{ill|Yamazu Shrine (Hamamatsu)|ja|山住神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamazu Shrine (Hamamatsu)|qq=|1=Yamazumi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11466290}} (Hamamatsu, Shizuoka Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Sado)|ja|大山祇神社 (佐渡市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Sado)|qq=|1=Ōyamazumi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q124496789}} (Sado, Niigata Prefecture) - Former Prefectural shrine; associated with the [[Sado Gold Mine]]. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)|ja|大山祇神社 (伊勢市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)|qq=}} (Ise, Mie Prefecture) - A subsidiary shrine within the [[Ise Grand Shrines]] precincts. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nantan)|ja|大山祇神社 (南丹市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nantan)|qq=}} (Nantan, Kyoto Prefecture) - Not dedicated to Ōyamazumi. * {{ill|Bekkyū Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|別宮大山祇神社|1=Bekkyū Ōyamazumi Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bekkyū Ōyamazumi Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Betsugu Oyamatsumi Shrine|qq=}} (Bekkyū, Imabari, Ehime Prefecture) - Former Prefectural shrine. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社 (今治市大三島)|Ōyamazumi Shrine=Ōyamazumi Shrine|lt=Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ōmishima)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Head shrine of all Ōyamazumi shrines nationwide. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishi no Tani)|ja|大山祇神社 (西の谷)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishi no Tani)|qq=}} (Niihama, Ehime Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)|ja|大山積神社 (新居浜市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)|qq=}} (Niihama, Ehime Prefecture) - Associated with the [[Besshi Copper Mine]]. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Michidoi, Shinkamigoto)|ja|大山祇神社 (新上五島町道土井郷)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Michidoi, Shinkamigoto)|qq=}} (Dōdoigō, Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Imazatogō)|ja|大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Imazatogō)|qq=}} (Imazatogō, Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Saikai)|ja|大山祇神社 (西海市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Saikai)|qq=}} (Saikai, Nagasaki Prefecture) - Associated with the [[Nishisonogi Coalfield]].

Ōyamazumi Shrine is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] dedicated to [[:en:Ōyamatsumi|Ōyamatsumi]], associated with the [[Mishima–Ōyamazumi cult]]. As of 1973, 897 shrines existed across Japan, from [[:en:Hokkaidō|Hokkaidō]] to [[:en:Kagoshima Prefecture|Kagoshima Prefecture]]. According to a 1995 survey by the Association of Shinto Shrines, there were 293 shrines, while a 2019 study by [[:en:Kanazawa Institute of Technology|Kanazawa Institute of Technology]] reported 960 shrines. The shrine may also be written as '''Ōyamazumi Shrine''', '''Ōyamatsumi Shrine''', '''Yamazumi Shrine''', or '''Yamatsumi Shrine'''.
The head shrine is the {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社 (今治市大三島)|Ōyamazumi Shrine=Ōyamazumi Shrine|lt=Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ōmishima)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Ōmishima|Ōmishima]], [[:en:Imabari|Imabari]], [[:en:Ehime Prefecture|Ehime Prefecture]]. Other related shrine names under the [[Mishima–Ōyamazumi cult]] include {{ill|Mishima Taisha|lt=Mishima Taisha|lt=Mishima Shrine|ja|三島神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishima Shrine|qq=}}.

associated with the [[Sado Gold Mine]]. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)|ja|大山祇神社 (伊勢市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)|qq=}} (Ise, Mie Prefecture) - A subsidiary shrine within the [[Ise Grand Shrines]] precincts. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nantan)|ja|大山祇神社 (南丹市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nantan)|qq=}} (Nantan, Kyoto Prefecture) - Not dedicated to Ōyamazumi. * {{ill|Bekkyū Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|別宮大山祇神社|1=Bekkyū Ōyamazumi Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bekkyū Ōyamazumi Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Betsugu Oyamatsumi Shrine|qq=}} (Bekkyū, Imabari, Ehime Prefecture) - Former Prefectural shrine. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社 (今治市大三島)|Ōyamazumi Shrine=Ōyamazumi Shrine|lt=Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ōmishima)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Head shrine of all Ōyamazumi shrines nationwide. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishi no Tani)|ja|大山祇神社 (西の谷)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishi no Tani)|qq=}} (Niihama, Ehime Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)|ja|大山積神社 (新居浜市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)|qq=}} (Niihama, Ehime Prefecture) - Associated with the [[Besshi Copper Mine]]. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Michidoi, Shinkamigoto)|ja|大山祇神社 (新上五島町道土井郷)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Michidoi, Shinkamigoto)|qq=}} (Dōdoigō, Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Imazatogō)|ja|大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Imazatogō)|qq=}} (Imazatogō, Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Saikai)|ja|大山祇神社 (西海市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Saikai)|qq=}} (Saikai, Nagasaki Prefecture) - Associated with the [[Nishisonogi Coalfield]].

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大山祇神社 (曖昧さ回避)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q16278443]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:43, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社, Ōyamazumi Jinja)
Ōyamazumi Shrine is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] dedicated to [[:en:Ōyamatsumi|Ōyamatsumi]], associated with the [[Mishima–Ōyamazumi cult]]. As of 1973, 897 shrines existed across Japan, from [[:en:Hokkaidō|Hokkaidō]] to [[:en:Kagoshima Prefecture|Kagoshima Prefecture]]. According to a 1995 survey by the Association of Shinto Shrines, there were 293 shrines, while a 2019 study by [[:en:Kanazawa Institute of Technology|Kanazawa Institute of Technology]] reported 960 shrines. The shrine may also be written as '''Ōyamazumi Shrine''', '''Ōyamatsumi Shrine''', '''Yamazumi Shrine''', or '''Yamatsumi Shrine'''.
The head shrine is the {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社 (今治市大三島)|Ōyamazumi Shrine=Ōyamazumi Shrine|lt=Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ōmishima)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Ōmishima|Ōmishima]], [[:en:Imabari|Imabari]], [[:en:Ehime Prefecture|Ehime Prefecture]]. Other related shrine names under the [[Mishima–Ōyamazumi cult]] include {{ill|Mishima Taisha|lt=Mishima Taisha|lt=Mishima Shrine|ja|三島神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishima Shrine|qq=}}.
== Shrines with Articles == * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishiaizu)|ja|大山祇神社 (西会津町)|1=Oyamatsumi Shrine (Nishi Aizu)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishiaizu)|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyamatsumi Shrine (Nishi Aizu)|qq=}} (Nishiaizu, Fukushima Prefecture) * {{ill|Yamatsumi Shrine (Iitate)|ja|山津見神社 (飯舘村)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatsumi Shrine (Iitate)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 山津見神社|1=Yamatsumi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11469987}} (Iitate, Fukushima Prefecture) * {{ill|Yamazu Shrine (Hamamatsu)|ja|山住神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamazu Shrine (Hamamatsu)|1=Yamazumi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11466290}} (Hamamatsu, Shizuoka Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Sado)|ja|大山祇神社 (佐渡市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Sado)|1=Ōyamazumi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q124496789}} (Sado, Niigata Prefecture) - Former Prefectural shrine; associated with the [[Sado Gold Mine]]. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)|ja|大山祇神社 (伊勢市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)|qq=}} (Ise, Mie Prefecture) - A subsidiary shrine within the [[Ise Grand Shrines]] precincts. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nantan)|ja|大山祇神社 (南丹市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nantan)|qq=}} (Nantan, Kyoto Prefecture) - Not dedicated to Ōyamazumi. * {{ill|Bekkyū Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|別宮大山祇神社|1=Bekkyū Ōyamazumi Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bekkyū Ōyamazumi Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Betsugu Oyamatsumi Shrine|qq=}} (Bekkyū, Imabari, Ehime Prefecture) - Former Prefectural shrine. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社 (今治市大三島)|Ōyamazumi Shrine=Ōyamazumi Shrine|lt=Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ōmishima)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Head shrine of all Ōyamazumi shrines nationwide. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishi no Tani)|ja|大山祇神社 (西の谷)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishi no Tani)|qq=}} (Niihama, Ehime Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)|ja|大山積神社 (新居浜市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)|qq=}} (Niihama, Ehime Prefecture) - Associated with the [[Besshi Copper Mine]]. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Michidoi, Shinkamigoto)|ja|大山祇神社 (新上五島町道土井郷)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Michidoi, Shinkamigoto)|qq=}} (Dōdoigō, Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Imazatogō)|ja|大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Imazatogō)|qq=}} (Imazatogō, Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Saikai)|ja|大山祇神社 (西海市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Saikai)|qq=}} (Saikai, Nagasaki Prefecture) - Associated with the [[Nishisonogi Coalfield]].

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大山祇神社 (曖昧さ回避)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q16278443]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:43, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社, Ōyamazumi Jinja)
Ōyamazumi Shrine is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] dedicated to [[:en:Ōyamatsumi|Ōyamatsumi]], associated with the [[Mishima–Ōyamazumi cult]]. As of 1973, 897 shrines existed across Japan, from [[:en:Hokkaidō|Hokkaidō]] to [[:en:Kagoshima Prefecture|Kagoshima Prefecture]]. According to a 1995 survey by the Association of Shinto Shrines, there were 293 shrines, while a 2019 study by [[:en:Kanazawa Institute of Technology|Kanazawa Institute of Technology]] reported 960 shrines. The shrine may also be written as '''Ōyamazumi Shrine''', '''Ōyamatsumi Shrine''', '''Yamazumi Shrine''', or '''Yamatsumi Shrine'''.
The head shrine is the {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社 (今治市大三島)|Ōyamazumi Shrine=Ōyamazumi Shrine|lt=Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ōmishima)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Ōmishima|Ōmishima]], [[:en:Imabari|Imabari]], [[:en:Ehime Prefecture|Ehime Prefecture]]. Other related shrine names under the [[Mishima–Ōyamazumi cult]] include {{ill|Mishima Taisha|lt=Mishima Taisha|lt=Mishima Shrine|ja|三島神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishima Shrine|qq=}}.
== Shrines with Articles == * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishiaizu)|ja|大山祇神社 (西会津町)|1=Oyamatsumi Shrine (Nishi Aizu)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishiaizu)|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyamatsumi Shrine (Nishi Aizu)|qq=}} (Nishiaizu, Fukushima Prefecture) * {{ill|Yamatsumi Shrine (Iitate)|ja|山津見神社 (飯舘村)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatsumi Shrine (Iitate)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 山津見神社|1=Yamatsumi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11469987}} (Iitate, Fukushima Prefecture) * {{ill|Yamazu Shrine (Hamamatsu)|ja|山住神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamazu Shrine (Hamamatsu)|1=Yamazumi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11466290}} (Hamamatsu, Shizuoka Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Sado)|ja|大山祇神社 (佐渡市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Sado)|1=Ōyamazumi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q124496789}} (Sado, Niigata Prefecture) - Former Prefectural shrine; <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大山祇神社 (曖昧さ回避)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q16278443]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:43, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社, Ōyamazumi Jinja)
Ōyamazumi Shrine is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] dedicated to [[:en:Ōyamatsumi|Ōyamatsumi]], associated with the [[Mishima–Ōyamazumi cult]]. As of 1973, 897 shrines existed across Japan, from [[:en:Hokkaidō|Hokkaidō]] to [[:en:Kagoshima Prefecture|Kagoshima Prefecture]]. According to a 1995 survey by the Association of Shinto Shrines, there were 293 shrines, while a 2019 study by [[:en:Kanazawa Institute of Technology|Kanazawa Institute of Technology]] reported 960 shrines. The shrine may also be written as '''Ōyamazumi Shrine''', '''Ōyamatsumi Shrine''', '''Yamazumi Shrine''', or '''Yamatsumi Shrine'''.
The head shrine is the {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社 (今治市大三島)|Ōyamazumi Shrine=Ōyamazumi Shrine|lt=Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ōmishima)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Ōmishima|Ōmishima]], [[:en:Imabari|Imabari]], [[:en:Ehime Prefecture|Ehime Prefecture]]. Other related shrine names under the [[Mishima–Ōyamazumi cult]] include {{ill|Mishima Taisha|lt=Mishima Taisha|lt=Mishima Shrine|ja|三島神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishima Shrine|qq=}}.
== Shrines with Articles == * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishiaizu)|ja|大山祇神社 (西会津町)|1=Oyamatsumi Shrine (Nishi Aizu)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishiaizu)|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyamatsumi Shrine (Nishi Aizu)|qq=}} (Nishiaizu, Fukushima Prefecture) * {{ill|Yamatsumi Shrine (Iitate)|ja|山津見神社 (飯舘村)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatsumi Shrine (Iitate)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 山津見神社|1=Yamatsumi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11469987}} (Iitate, Fukushima Prefecture) * {{ill|Yamazu Shrine (Hamamatsu)|ja|山住神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamazu Shrine (Hamamatsu)|1=Yamazumi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11466290}} (Hamamatsu, Shizuoka Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Sado)|ja|大山祇神社 (佐渡市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Sado)|1=Ōyamazumi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q124496789}} (Sado, Niigata Prefecture) - Former Prefectural shrine; associated with the [[Sado Gold Mine]]. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)|ja|大山祇神社 (伊勢市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)|qq=}} (Ise, Mie Prefecture) - A subsidiary shrine within the [[Ise Grand Shrines]] precincts. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nantan)|ja|大山祇神社 (南丹市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nantan)|qq=}} (Nantan, Kyoto Prefecture) - Not dedicated to Ōyamazumi. * {{ill|Bekkyū Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|別宮大山祇神社|1=Bekkyū Ōyamazumi Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bekkyū Ōyamazumi Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Betsugu Oyamatsumi Shrine|qq=}} (Bekkyū, Imabari, Ehime Prefecture) - Former Prefectural shrine. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社 (今治市大三島)|Ōyamazumi Shrine=Ōyamazumi Shrine|lt=Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ōmishima)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Head shrine of all Ōyamazumi shrines nationwide. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishi no Tani)|ja|大山祇神社 (西の谷)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishi no Tani)|qq=}} (Niihama, Ehime Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)|ja|大山積神社 (新居浜市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)|qq=}} (Niihama, Ehime Prefecture) - Associated with the [[Besshi Copper Mine]]. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Michidoi, Shinkamigoto)|ja|大山祇神社 (新上五島町道土井郷)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Michidoi, Shinkamigoto)|qq=}} (Dōdoigō, Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Imazatogō)|ja|大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Imazatogō)|qq=}} (Imazatogō, Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Saikai)|ja|大山祇神社 (西海市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Saikai)|qq=}} (Saikai, Nagasaki Prefecture) - Associated with the [[Nishisonogi Coalfield]].

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大山祇神社 (曖昧さ回避)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q16278443]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:43, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社, Ōyamazumi Jinja)
Ōyamazumi Shrine is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] dedicated to [[:en:Ōyamatsumi|Ōyamatsumi]], associated with the [[Mishima–Ōyamazumi cult]]. As of 1973, 897 shrines existed across Japan, from [[:en:Hokkaidō|Hokkaidō]] to [[:en:Kagoshima Prefecture|Kagoshima Prefecture]]. According to a 1995 survey by the Association of Shinto Shrines, there were 293 shrines, while a 2019 study by [[:en:Kanazawa Institute of Technology|Kanazawa Institute of Technology]] reported 960 shrines. The shrine may also be written as '''Ōyamazumi Shrine''', '''Ōyamatsumi Shrine''', '''Yamazumi Shrine''', or '''Yamatsumi Shrine'''.
The head shrine is the {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社 (今治市大三島)|Ōyamazumi Shrine=Ōyamazumi Shrine|lt=Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ōmishima)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Ōmishima|Ōmishima]], [[:en:Imabari|Imabari]], [[:en:Ehime Prefecture|Ehime Prefecture]]. Other related shrine names under the [[Mishima–Ōyamazumi cult]] include {{ill|Mishima Taisha|lt=Mishima Taisha|lt=Mishima Shrine|ja|三島神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishima Shrine|qq=}}.
== Shrines with Articles == * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishiaizu)|ja|大山祇神社 (西会津町)|1=Oyamatsumi Shrine (Nishi Aizu)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishiaizu)|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyamatsumi Shrine (Nishi Aizu)|qq=}} (Nishiaizu, Fukushima Prefecture) * {{ill|Yamatsumi Shrine (Iitate)|ja|山津見神社 (飯舘村)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatsumi Shrine (Iitate)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 山津見神社|1=Yamatsumi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11469987}} (Iitate, Fukushima Prefecture) * {{ill|Yamazu Shrine (Hamamatsu)|ja|山住神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamazu Shrine (Hamamatsu)|1=Yamazumi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11466290}} (Hamamatsu, Shizuoka Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Sado)|ja|大山祇神社 (佐渡市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Sado)|1=Ōyamazumi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q124496789}} (Sado, Niigata Prefecture) - Former Prefectural shrine; associated with the [[Sado Gold Mine]]. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)|ja|大山祇神社 (伊勢市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)|qq=}} (Ise, Mie Prefecture) - A subsidiary shrine within the [[Ise Grand Shrines]] precincts. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nantan)|ja|大山祇神社 (南丹市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nantan)|qq=}} (Nantan, Kyoto Prefecture) - Not dedicated to Ōyamazumi. * {{ill|Bekkyū Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|別宮大山祇神社|1=Bekkyū Ōyamazumi Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bekkyū Ōyamazumi Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Betsugu Oyamatsumi Shrine|qq=}} (Bekkyū, Imabari, Ehime Prefecture) - Former Prefectural shrine. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社 (今治市大三島)|Ōyamazumi Shrine=Ōyamazumi Shrine|lt=Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ōmishima)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Head shrine of all Ōyamazumi shrines nationwide. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishi no Tani)|ja|大山祇神社 (西の谷)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishi no Tani)|qq=}} (Niihama, Ehime Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)|ja|大山積神社 (新居浜市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)|qq=}} (Niihama, Ehime Prefecture) - Associated with the [[Besshi Copper Mine]]. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Michidoi, Shinkamigoto)|ja|大山祇神社 (新上五島町道土井郷)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Michidoi, Shinkamigoto)|qq=}} (Dōdoigō, Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Imazatogō)|ja|大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Imazatogō)|qq=}} (Imazatogō, Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Saikai)|ja|大山祇神社 (西海市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Saikai)|qq=}} (Saikai, Nagasaki Prefecture) - Associated with the [[Nishisonogi Coalfield]].

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大山祇神社 (曖昧さ回避)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大山祇神社 (曖昧さ回避)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大山祇神社 (曖昧さ回避)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q16278443]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:43, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社, Ōyamazumi Jinja)
Ōyamazumi Shrine is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] dedicated to [[:en:Ōyamatsumi|Ōyamatsumi]], associated with the [[Mishima–Ōyamazumi cult]]. As of 1973, 897 shrines existed across Japan, from [[:en:Hokkaidō|Hokkaidō]] to [[:en:Kagoshima Prefecture|Kagoshima Prefecture]]. According to a 1995 survey by the Association of Shinto Shrines, there were 293 shrines, while a 2019 study by [[:en:Kanazawa Institute of Technology|Kanazawa Institute of Technology]] reported 960 shrines. The shrine may also be written as '''Ōyamazumi Shrine''', '''Ōyamatsumi Shrine''', '''Yamazumi Shrine''', or '''Yamatsumi Shrine'''.
The head shrine is the {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社 (今治市大三島)|Ōyamazumi Shrine=Ōyamazumi Shrine|lt=Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ōmishima)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Ōmishima|Ōmishima]], [[:en:Imabari|Imabari]], [[:en:Ehime Prefecture|Ehime Prefecture]]. Other related shrine names under the [[Mishima–Ōyamazumi cult]] include {{ill|Mishima Taisha|lt=Mishima Taisha|lt=Mishima Shrine|ja|三島神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishima Shrine|qq=}}.
== Shrines with Articles == * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishiaizu)|ja|大山祇神社 (西会津町)|1=Oyamatsumi Shrine (Nishi Aizu)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishiaizu)|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyamatsumi Shrine (Nishi Aizu)|qq=}} (Nishiaizu, Fukushima Prefecture) * {{ill|Yamatsumi Shrine (Iitate)|ja|山津見神社 (飯舘村)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatsumi Shrine (Iitate)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 山津見神社|1=Yamatsumi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11469987}} (Iitate, Fukushima Prefecture) * {{ill|Yamazu Shrine (Hamamatsu)|ja|山住神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamazu Shrine (Hamamatsu)|1=Yamazumi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11466290}} (Hamamatsu, Shizuoka Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Sado)|ja|大山祇神社 (佐渡市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Sado)|1=Ōyamazumi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q124496789}} (Sado, Niigata Prefecture) - Former Prefectural shrine; associated with the [[Sado Gold Mine]]. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)|ja|大山祇神社 (伊勢市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)|qq=}} (Ise, Mie Prefecture) - A subsidiary shrine within the [[Ise Grand Shrines]] precincts. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nantan)|ja|大山祇神社 (南丹市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nantan)|qq=}} (Nantan, Kyoto Prefecture) - Not dedicated to Ōyamazumi. * {{ill|Bekkyū Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|別宮大山祇神社|1=Bekkyū Ōyamazumi Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bekkyū Ōyamazumi Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Betsugu Oyamatsumi Shrine|qq=}} (Bekkyū, Imabari, Ehime Prefecture) - Former Prefectural shrine. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社 (今治市大三島)|Ōyamazumi Shrine=Ōyamazumi Shrine|lt=Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ōmishima)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Head shrine of all Ōyamazumi shrines nationwide. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishi no Tani)|ja|大山祇神社 (西の谷)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishi no Tani)|qq=}} (Niihama, Ehime Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)|ja|大山積神社 (新居浜市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)|qq=}} (Niihama, Ehime Prefecture) - Associated with the [[Besshi Copper Mine]]. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Michidoi, Shinkamigoto)|ja|大山祇神社 (新上五島町道土井郷)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Michidoi, Shinkamigoto)|qq=}} (Dōdoigō, Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Imazatogō)|ja|大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Imazatogō)|qq=}} (Imazatogō, Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Saikai)|ja|大山祇神社 (西海市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Saikai)|qq=}} (Saikai, Nagasaki Prefecture) - Associated with the [[Nishisonogi Coalfield]].

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大山祇神社 (曖昧さ回避)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大山祇神社 (曖昧さ回避)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大山祇神社 (曖昧さ回避)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q16278443]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:43, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社, Ōyamazumi Jinja)
Ōyamazumi Shrine is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] dedicated to [[:en:Ōyamatsumi|Ōyamatsumi]], associated with the [[Mishima–Ōyamazumi cult]]. As of 1973, 897 shrines existed across Japan, from [[:en:Hokkaidō|Hokkaidō]] to [[:en:Kagoshima Prefecture|Kagoshima Prefecture]]. According to a 1995 survey by the Association of Shinto Shrines, there were 293 shrines, while a 2019 study by [[:en:Kanazawa Institute of Technology|Kanazawa Institute of Technology]] reported 960 shrines. The shrine may also be written as '''Ōyamazumi Shrine''', '''Ōyamatsumi Shrine''', '''Yamazumi Shrine''', or '''Yamatsumi Shrine'''.
The head shrine is the {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社 (今治市大三島)|Ōyamazumi Shrine=Ōyamazumi Shrine|lt=Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ōmishima)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Ōmishima|Ōmishima]], [[:en:Imabari|Imabari]], [[:en:Ehime Prefecture|Ehime Prefecture]]. Other related shrine names under the [[Mishima–Ōyamazumi cult]] include {{ill|Mishima Taisha|lt=Mishima Taisha|lt=Mishima Shrine|ja|三島神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishima Shrine|qq=}}.
== Shrines with Articles == * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishiaizu)|ja|大山祇神社 (西会津町)|1=Oyamatsumi Shrine (Nishi Aizu)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishiaizu)|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyamatsumi Shrine (Nishi Aizu)|qq=}} (Nishiaizu, Fukushima Prefecture) * {{ill|Yamatsumi Shrine (Iitate)|ja|山津見神社 (飯舘村)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatsumi Shrine (Iitate)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 山津見神社|1=Yamatsumi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11469987}} (Iitate, Fukushima Prefecture) * {{ill|Yamazu Shrine (Hamamatsu)|ja|山住神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamazu Shrine (Hamamatsu)|1=Yamazumi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11466290}} (Hamamatsu, Shizuoka Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Sado)|ja|大山祇神社 (佐渡市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Sado)|1=Ōyamazumi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q124496789}} (Sado, Niigata Prefecture) - Former Prefectural shrine; associated with the [[Sado Gold Mine]]. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)|ja|大山祇神社 (伊勢市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)|qq=}} (Ise, Mie Prefecture) - A subsidiary shrine within the [[Ise Grand Shrines]] precincts. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nantan)|ja|大山祇神社 (南丹市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nantan)|qq=}} (Nantan, Kyoto Prefecture) - Not dedicated to Ōyamazumi. * {{ill|Bekkyū Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|別宮大山祇神社|1=Bekkyū Ōyamazumi Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bekkyū Ōyamazumi Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Betsugu Oyamatsumi Shrine|qq=}} (Bekkyū, Imabari, Ehime Prefecture) - Former Prefectural shrine. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社 (今治市大三島)|Ōyamazumi Shrine=Ōyamazumi Shrine|lt=Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ōmishima)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Head shrine of all Ōyamazumi shrines nationwide. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishi no Tani)|ja|大山祇神社 (西の谷)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishi no Tani)|qq=}} (Niihama, Ehime Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)|ja|大山積神社 (新居浜市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)|qq=}} (Niihama, Ehime Prefecture) - Associated with the [[Besshi Copper Mine]]. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Michidoi, Shinkamigoto)|ja|大山祇神社 (新上五島町道土井郷)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Michidoi, Shinkamigoto)|qq=}} (Dōdoigō, Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Imazatogō)|ja|大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Imazatogō)|qq=}} (Imazatogō, Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Saikai)|ja|大山祇神社 (西海市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Saikai)|qq=}} (Saikai, Nagasaki Prefecture) - Associated with the [[Nishisonogi Coalfield]].

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大山祇神社 (曖昧さ回避)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大山祇神社 (曖昧さ回避)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大山祇神社 (曖昧さ回避)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q16278443]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:43, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社, Ōyamazumi Jinja)
Ōyamazumi Shrine is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] dedicated to [[:en:Ōyamatsumi|Ōyamatsumi]], associated with the [[Mishima–Ōyamazumi cult]]. As of 1973, 897 shrines existed across Japan, from [[:en:Hokkaidō|Hokkaidō]] to [[:en:Kagoshima Prefecture|Kagoshima Prefecture]]. According to a 1995 survey by the Association of Shinto Shrines, there were 293 shrines, while a 2019 study by [[:en:Kanazawa Institute of Technology|Kanazawa Institute of Technology]] reported 960 shrines. The shrine may also be written as '''Ōyamazumi Shrine''', '''Ōyamatsumi Shrine''', '''Yamazumi Shrine''', or '''Yamatsumi Shrine'''.
The head shrine is the {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社 (今治市大三島)|Ōyamazumi Shrine=Ōyamazumi Shrine|lt=Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ōmishima)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Ōmishima|Ōmishima]], [[:en:Imabari|Imabari]], [[:en:Ehime Prefecture|Ehime Prefecture]]. Other related shrine names under the [[Mishima–Ōyamazumi cult]] include {{ill|Mishima Taisha|lt=Mishima Taisha|lt=Mishima Shrine|ja|三島神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishima Shrine|qq=}}.

associated with the [[Sado Gold Mine]]. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)|ja|大山祇神社 (伊勢市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)|qq=}} (Ise, Mie Prefecture) - A subsidiary shrine within the [[Ise Grand Shrines]] precincts. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nantan)|ja|大山祇神社 (南丹市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nantan)|qq=}} (Nantan, Kyoto Prefecture) - Not dedicated to Ōyamazumi. * {{ill|Bekkyū Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|別宮大山祇神社|1=Bekkyū Ōyamazumi Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bekkyū Ōyamazumi Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Betsugu Oyamatsumi Shrine|qq=}} (Bekkyū, Imabari, Ehime Prefecture) - Former Prefectural shrine. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社 (今治市大三島)|Ōyamazumi Shrine=Ōyamazumi Shrine|lt=Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ōmishima)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Head shrine of all Ōyamazumi shrines nationwide. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishi no Tani)|ja|大山祇神社 (西の谷)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishi no Tani)|qq=}} (Niihama, Ehime Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)|ja|大山積神社 (新居浜市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)|qq=}} (Niihama, Ehime Prefecture) - Associated with the [[Besshi Copper Mine]]. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Michidoi, Shinkamigoto)|ja|大山祇神社 (新上五島町道土井郷)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Michidoi, Shinkamigoto)|qq=}} (Dōdoigō, Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Imazatogō)|ja|大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Imazatogō)|qq=}} (Imazatogō, Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Saikai)|ja|大山祇神社 (西海市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Saikai)|qq=}} (Saikai, Nagasaki Prefecture) - Associated with the [[Nishisonogi Coalfield]].

revision.comment: v25: Convert interwiki links [[:en:...]] to {{ill}} templates
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大山祇神社 (曖昧さ回避)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大山祇神社 (曖昧さ回避)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大山祇神社 (曖昧さ回避)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q16278443]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:43, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社, Ōyamazumi Jinja)
Ōyamazumi Shrine is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} dedicated to {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|en|Ōyamatsumi|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi}}, associated with the [[Mishima–Ōyamazumi cult]]. As of 1973, 897 shrines existed across Japan, from [[:en:Hokkaidō|Hokkaidō]] to {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|en|Kagoshima Prefecture|WD=Q15701|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture}}. According to a 1995 survey by the Association of Shinto Shrines, there were 293 shrines, while a 2019 study by {{ill|Kanazawa Institute of Technology|en|Kanazawa Institute of Technology|WD=Q6360885|lt=Kanazawa Institute of Technology}} reported 960 shrines. The shrine may also be written as '''Ōyamazumi Shrine''', '''Ōyamatsumi Shrine''', '''Yamazumi Shrine''', or '''Yamatsumi Shrine'''.
The head shrine is the {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社 (今治市大三島)|Ōyamazumi Shrine=Ōyamazumi Shrine|lt=Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ōmishima)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Ōmishima|Ōmishima]], [[:en:Imabari|Imabari]], {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|en|Ehime Prefecture|WD=Q123376|lt=Ehime Prefecture}}. Other related shrine names under the [[Mishima–Ōyamazumi cult]] include {{ill|Mishima Taisha|lt=Mishima Taisha|lt=Mishima Shrine|ja|三島神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishima Shrine|qq=}}.
== Shrines with Articles == * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishiaizu)|ja|大山祇神社 (西会津町)|1=Oyamatsumi Shrine (Nishi Aizu)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishiaizu)|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyamatsumi Shrine (Nishi Aizu)|qq=}} (Nishiaizu, Fukushima Prefecture) * {{ill|Yamatsumi Shrine (Iitate)|ja|山津見神社 (飯舘村)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatsumi Shrine (Iitate)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 山津見神社|1=Yamatsumi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11469987}} (Iitate, Fukushima Prefecture) * {{ill|Yamazu Shrine (Hamamatsu)|ja|山住神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamazu Shrine (Hamamatsu)|1=Yamazumi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11466290}} (Hamamatsu, Shizuoka Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Sado)|ja|大山祇神社 (佐渡市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Sado)|1=Ōyamazumi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q124496789}} (Sado, Niigata Prefecture) - Former Prefectural shrine; associated with the [[Sado Gold Mine]]. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)|ja|大山祇神社 (伊勢市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)|qq=}} (Ise, Mie Prefecture) - A subsidiary shrine within the [[Ise Grand Shrines]] precincts. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nantan)|ja|大山祇神社 (南丹市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nantan)|qq=}} (Nantan, Kyoto Prefecture) - Not dedicated to Ōyamazumi. * {{ill|Bekkyū Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|別宮大山祇神社|1=Bekkyū Ōyamazumi Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bekkyū Ōyamazumi Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Betsugu Oyamatsumi Shrine|qq=}} (Bekkyū, Imabari, Ehime Prefecture) - Former Prefectural shrine. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社 (今治市大三島)|Ōyamazumi Shrine=Ōyamazumi Shrine|lt=Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ōmishima)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Head shrine of all Ōyamazumi shrines nationwide. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishi no Tani)|ja|大山祇神社 (西の谷)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishi no Tani)|qq=}} (Niihama, Ehime Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)|ja|大山積神社 (新居浜市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)|qq=}} (Niihama, Ehime Prefecture) - Associated with the [[Besshi Copper Mine]]. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Michidoi, Shinkamigoto)|ja|大山祇神社 (新上五島町道土井郷)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Michidoi, Shinkamigoto)|qq=}} (Dōdoigō, Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Imazatogō)|ja|大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Imazatogō)|qq=}} (Imazatogō, Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Saikai)|ja|大山祇神社 (西海市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Saikai)|qq=}} (Saikai, Nagasaki Prefecture) - Associated with the [[Nishisonogi Coalfield]].

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大山祇神社 (曖昧さ回避)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大山祇神社 (曖昧さ回避)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大山祇神社 (曖昧さ回避)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q16278443]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:43, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社, Ōyamazumi Jinja)
Ōyamazumi Shrine is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} dedicated to {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|en|Ōyamatsumi|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi}}, associated with the [[Mishima–Ōyamazumi cult]]. As of 1973, 897 shrines existed across Japan, from [[:en:Hokkaidō|Hokkaidō]] to {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|en|Kagoshima Prefecture|WD=Q15701|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture}}. According to a 1995 survey by the Association of Shinto Shrines, there were 293 shrines, while a 2019 study by {{ill|Kanazawa Institute of Technology|en|Kanazawa Institute of Technology|WD=Q6360885|lt=Kanazawa Institute of Technology}} reported 960 shrines. The shrine may also be written as '''Ōyamazumi Shrine''', '''Ōyamatsumi Shrine''', '''Yamazumi Shrine''', or '''Yamatsumi Shrine'''.
The head shrine is the {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社 (今治市大三島)|Ōyamazumi Shrine=Ōyamazumi Shrine|lt=Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ōmishima)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Ōmishima|Ōmishima]], [[:en:Imabari|Imabari]], {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|en|Ehime Prefecture|WD=Q123376|lt=Ehime Prefecture}}. Other related shrine names under the [[Mishima–Ōyamazumi cult]] include {{ill|Mishima Taisha|lt=Mishima Taisha|lt=Mishima Shrine|ja|三島神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishima Shrine|qq=}}.
== Shrines with Articles == * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishiaizu)|ja|大山祇神社 (西会津町)|1=Oyamatsumi Shrine (Nishi Aizu)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishiaizu)|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyamatsumi Shrine (Nishi Aizu)|qq=}} (Nishiaizu, Fukushima Prefecture) * {{ill|Yamatsumi Shrine (Iitate)|ja|山津見神社 (飯舘村)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatsumi Shrine (Iitate)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 山津見神社|1=Yamatsumi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11469987}} (Iitate, Fukushima Prefecture) * {{ill|Yamazu Shrine (Hamamatsu)|ja|山住神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamazu Shrine (Hamamatsu)|1=Yamazumi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11466290}} (Hamamatsu, Shizuoka Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Sado)|ja|大山祇神社 (佐渡市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Sado)|1=Ōyamazumi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q124496789}} (Sado, Niigata Prefecture) - Former Prefectural shrine; associated with the [[Sado Gold Mine]]. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)|ja|大山祇神社 (伊勢市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)|qq=}} (Ise, Mie Prefecture) - A subsidiary shrine within the [[Ise Grand Shrines]] precincts. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nantan)|ja|大山祇神社 (南丹市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nantan)|qq=}} (Nantan, Kyoto Prefecture) - Not dedicated to Ōyamazumi. * {{ill|Bekkyū Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|別宮大山祇神社|1=Bekkyū Ōyamazumi Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bekkyū Ōyamazumi Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Betsugu Oyamatsumi Shrine|qq=}} (Bekkyū, Imabari, Ehime Prefecture) - Former Prefectural shrine. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社 (今治市大三島)|Ōyamazumi Shrine=Ōyamazumi Shrine|lt=Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ōmishima)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Head shrine of all Ōyamazumi shrines nationwide. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishi no Tani)|ja|大山祇神社 (西の谷)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishi no Tani)|qq=}} (Niihama, Ehime Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)|ja|大山積神社 (新居浜市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)|qq=}} (Niihama, Ehime Prefecture) - Associated with the [[Besshi Copper Mine]]. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Michidoi, Shinkamigoto)|ja|大山祇神社 (新上五島町道土井郷)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Michidoi, Shinkamigoto)|qq=}} (Dōdoigō, Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Imazatogō)|ja|大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Imazatogō)|qq=}} (Imazatogō, Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Saikai)|ja|大山祇神社 (西海市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Saikai)|qq=}} (Saikai, Nagasaki Prefecture) - Associated with the [[Nishisonogi Coalfield]].

As of 1973, 897 shrines existed across Japan, from [[:en:Hokkaidō|Hokkaidō]] to {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|en|Kagoshima Prefecture|WD=Q15701|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture}}. According to a 1995 survey by the Association of Shinto Shrines, there were 293 shrines, while a 2019 study by {{ill|Kanazawa Institute of Technology|en|Kanazawa Institute of Technology|WD=Q6360885|lt=Kanazawa Institute of Technology}} reported 960 shrines. The shrine may also be written as '''Ōyamazumi Shrine''', '''Ōyamatsumi Shrine''', '''Yamazumi Shrine''', or '''Yamatsumi Shrine'''.
The head shrine is the {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社 (今治市大三島)|Ōyamazumi Shrine=Ōyamazumi Shrine|lt=Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ōmishima)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Ōmishima|Ōmishima]], [[:en:Imabari|Imabari]], {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|en|Ehime Prefecture|WD=Q123376|lt=Ehime Prefecture}}. Other related shrine names under the [[Mishima–Ōyamazumi cult]] include {{ill|Mishima Taisha|lt=Mishima Taisha|lt=Mishima Shrine|ja|三島神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishima Shrine|qq=}}.

associated with the [[Sado Gold Mine]]. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)|ja|大山祇神社 (伊勢市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)|qq=}} (Ise, Mie Prefecture) - A subsidiary shrine within the [[Ise Grand Shrines]] precincts. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nantan)|ja|大山祇神社 (南丹市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nantan)|qq=}} (Nantan, Kyoto Prefecture) - Not dedicated to Ōyamazumi. * {{ill|Bekkyū Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|別宮大山祇神社|1=Bekkyū Ōyamazumi Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bekkyū Ōyamazumi Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Betsugu Oyamatsumi Shrine|qq=}} (Bekkyū, Imabari, Ehime Prefecture) - Former Prefectural shrine. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社 (今治市大三島)|Ōyamazumi Shrine=Ōyamazumi Shrine|lt=Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ōmishima)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Head shrine of all Ōyamazumi shrines nationwide. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishi no Tani)|ja|大山祇神社 (西の谷)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishi no Tani)|qq=}} (Niihama, Ehime Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)|ja|大山積神社 (新居浜市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)|qq=}} (Niihama, Ehime Prefecture) - Associated with the [[Besshi Copper Mine]]. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Michidoi, Shinkamigoto)|ja|大山祇神社 (新上五島町道土井郷)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Michidoi, Shinkamigoto)|qq=}} (Dōdoigō, Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Imazatogō)|ja|大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Imazatogō)|qq=}} (Imazatogō, Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Saikai)|ja|大山祇神社 (西海市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Saikai)|qq=}} (Saikai, Nagasaki Prefecture) - Associated with the [[Nishisonogi Coalfield]].

Ōyamazumi Shrine is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} dedicated to {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|en|Ōyamatsumi|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi}}, associated with the [[Mishima–Ōyamazumi cult]]. As of 1973, 897 shrines existed across Japan, from [[:en:Hokkaidō|Hokkaidō]] to {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|en|Kagoshima Prefecture|WD=Q15701|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture}}. According to a 1995 survey by the Association of Shinto Shrines, there were 293 shrines, while a 2019 study by {{ill|Kanazawa Institute of Technology|en|Kanazawa Institute of Technology|WD=Q6360885|lt=Kanazawa Institute of Technology}} reported 960 shrines. The shrine may also be written as '''Ōyamazumi Shrine''', '''Ōyamatsumi Shrine''', '''Yamazumi Shrine''', or '''Yamatsumi Shrine'''.
The head shrine is the {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社 (今治市大三島)|Ōyamazumi Shrine=Ōyamazumi Shrine|lt=Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ōmishima)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Ōmishima|Ōmishima]], [[:en:Imabari|Imabari]], {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|en|Ehime Prefecture|WD=Q123376|lt=Ehime Prefecture}}. Other related shrine names under the [[Mishima–Ōyamazumi cult]] include {{ill|Mishima Taisha|lt=Mishima Taisha|lt=Mishima Shrine|ja|三島神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishima Shrine|qq=}}.
== Shrines with Articles == * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishiaizu)|ja|大山祇神社 (西会津町)|1=Oyamatsumi Shrine (Nishi Aizu)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishiaizu)|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyamatsumi Shrine (Nishi Aizu)|qq=}} (Nishiaizu, Fukushima Prefecture) * {{ill|Yamatsumi Shrine (Iitate)|ja|山津見神社 (飯舘村)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatsumi Shrine (Iitate)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 山津見神社|1=Yamatsumi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11469987}} (Iitate, Fukushima Prefecture) * {{ill|Yamazu Shrine (Hamamatsu)|ja|山住神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamazu Shrine (Hamamatsu)|1=Yamazumi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11466290}} (Hamamatsu, Shizuoka Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Sado)|ja|大山祇神社 (佐渡市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Sado)|1=Ōyamazumi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q124496789}} (Sado, Niigata Prefecture) - Former Prefectural shrine; associated with the [[Sado Gold Mine]]. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)|ja|大山祇神社 (伊勢市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)|qq=}} (Ise, Mie Prefecture) - A subsidiary shrine within the [[Ise Grand Shrines]] precincts. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nantan)|ja|大山祇神社 (南丹市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nantan)|qq=}} (Nantan, Kyoto Prefecture) - Not dedicated to Ōyamazumi. * {{ill|Bekkyū Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|別宮大山祇神社|1=Bekkyū Ōyamazumi Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bekkyū Ōyamazumi Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Betsugu Oyamatsumi Shrine|qq=}} (Bekkyū, Imabari, Ehime Prefecture) - Former Prefectural shrine. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社 (今治市大三島)|Ōyamazumi Shrine=Ōyamazumi Shrine|lt=Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ōmishima)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Head shrine of all Ōyamazumi shrines nationwide. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishi no Tani)|ja|大山祇神社 (西の谷)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishi no Tani)|qq=}} (Niihama, Ehime Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)|ja|大山積神社 (新居浜市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)|qq=}} (Niihama, Ehime Prefecture) - Associated with the [[Besshi Copper Mine]]. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Michidoi, Shinkamigoto)|ja|大山祇神社 (新上五島町道土井郷)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Michidoi, Shinkamigoto)|qq=}} (Dōdoigō, Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Imazatogō)|ja|大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Imazatogō)|qq=}} (Imazatogō, Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Saikai)|ja|大山祇神社 (西海市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Saikai)|qq=}} (Saikai, Nagasaki Prefecture) - Associated with the [[Nishisonogi Coalfield]].

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大山祇神社 (曖昧さ回避)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大山祇神社 (曖昧さ回避)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大山祇神社 (曖昧さ回避)]]
'''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社, Ōyamazumi Jinja)
Ōyamazumi Shrine is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} dedicated to {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|en|Ōyamatsumi|WD=Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|1=Ōyamatsumi}}, associated with the [[Mishima–Ōyamazumi cult]]. As of 1973, 897 shrines existed across Japan, from [[:en:Hokkaidō|Hokkaidō]] to {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|en|Kagoshima Prefecture|WD=Q15701|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture|1=Kagoshima Prefecture}}. According to a 1995 survey by the Association of Shinto Shrines, there were 293 shrines, while a 2019 study by {{ill|Kanazawa Institute of Technology|en|Kanazawa Institute of Technology|WD=Q6360885|lt=Kanazawa Institute of Technology|1=Kanazawa Institute of Technology}} reported 960 shrines. The shrine may also be written as '''Ōyamazumi Shrine''', '''Ōyamatsumi Shrine''', '''Yamazumi Shrine''', or '''Yamatsumi Shrine'''.
The head shrine is the {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社 (今治市大三島)|Ōyamazumi Shrine=Ōyamazumi Shrine|lt=Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ōmishima)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Ōmishima|Ōmishima]], [[:en:Imabari|Imabari]], {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|en|Ehime Prefecture|WD=Q123376|lt=Ehime Prefecture|1=Ehime Prefecture}}. Other related shrine names under the [[Mishima–Ōyamazumi cult]] include {{ill|Mishima Taisha|lt=Mishima Taisha|lt=Mishima Shrine|ja|三島神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishima Shrine|qq=}}.
== Shrines with Articles == * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishiaizu)|ja|大山祇神社 (西会津町)|1=Oyamatsumi Shrine (Nishi Aizu)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishiaizu)|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyamatsumi Shrine (Nishi Aizu)|qq=}} (Nishiaizu, Fukushima Prefecture) * {{ill|Yamatsumi Shrine (Iitate)|ja|山津見神社 (飯舘村)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatsumi Shrine (Iitate)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 山津見神社|1=Yamatsumi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11469987}} (Iitate, Fukushima Prefecture) * {{ill|Yamazu Shrine (Hamamatsu)|ja|山住神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamazu Shrine (Hamamatsu)|1=Yamazumi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11466290}} (Hamamatsu, Shizuoka Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Sado)|ja|大山祇神社 (佐渡市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Sado)|1=Oyamazumi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q124496789}} (Sado, Niigata Prefecture) - Former Prefectural shrine; associated with the [[Sado Gold Mine]]. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)|ja|大山祇神社 (伊勢市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)|qq=}} (Ise, Mie Prefecture) - A subsidiary shrine within the [[Ise Grand Shrines]] precincts. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nantan)|ja|大山祇神社 (南丹市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nantan)|qq=}} (Nantan, Kyoto Prefecture) - Not dedicated to Ōyamazumi. * {{ill|Bekkyū Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|別宮大山祇神社|1=Bekkyū Ōyamazumi Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bekkyū Ōyamazumi Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Betsugu Oyamatsumi Shrine|qq=}} (Bekkyū, Imabari, Ehime Prefecture) - Former Prefectural shrine. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社 (今治市大三島)|Ōyamazumi Shrine=Ōyamazumi Shrine|lt=Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ōmishima)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Head shrine of all Ōyamazumi shrines nationwide. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishi no Tani)|ja|大山祇神社 (西の谷)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishi no Tani)|qq=}} (Niihama, Ehime Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)|ja|大山積神社 (新居浜市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)|qq=}} (Niihama, Ehime Prefecture) - Associated with the [[Besshi Copper Mine]]. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Michidoi, Shinkamigoto)|ja|大山祇神社 (新上五島町道土井郷)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Michidoi, Shinkamigoto)|qq=}} (Dōdoigō, Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Imazatogō)|ja|大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Imazatogō)|qq=}} (Imazatogō, Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Saikai)|ja|大山祇神社 (西海市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamazumi Shrine (Saikai)|qq=}} (Saikai, Nagasaki Prefecture) - Associated with the [[Nishisonogi Coalfield]].
